spodumene.description: LiAlSi₂O₆ — monoclinic pyroxene. Flattened tabular prisms that read as 'book' shape, with two cleavage directions at ~87° producing characteristic parting. Can reach 14 meters — among the longest single crystals on Earth (Etta mine, South Dakota). Lithium accumulates late in pegmatite crystallization because no early mineral takes it, so spodumene is the residual-fluid signature. Kunzite (pink-lilac) from Mn²⁺ substitution shows strong pink-orange SW fluorescence. Hiddenite (green) from Cr³⁺ is rarer. Named for George Kunz, Tiffany's mineralogist, who bought Minas Gerais specimens by the crate.

trace_ingredients.Fe: yellow-brown cast in triphane

fluorescence.note: kunzite variety; hiddenite shows weaker orange-red

twin_laws.name: simple_100
twin_laws._source: Hawthorne (1981) Reviews in Mineralogy 9A — pyroxenes; Anthony Handbook v.II. Monoclinic pyroxene {100} simple twin is the standard pyroxene-group growth twin (same law as augite, diopside, enstatite). Reported in California + Afghanistan kunzite specimens at 5-10% frequency among euhedral crystals.

acid_dissolution.reaction: resistant to common acids

spodumene.audit_status: new (Minas Gerais Variant A, spodumene). First Li consumer — Li has been tracked as pegmatite trace since phase-2 but had no sink until now. Not yet in agent-api/vugg-agent.js.

habit_variants.name: purple_book
habit_variants.trigger: Mn>=2 ppm, σ>1.5, T>=400°C — hexagonal Mn-purple book (Cruzeiro/Himalaya/Pala classic)

habit_variants.name: gray_book
habit_variants.trigger: Fe>=50 ppm — gray-greenish book, transitional toward zinnwaldite

habit_variants.name: pale_book
habit_variants.trigger: low Mn + low Fe — pale yellow to colorless clean lepidolite

habit_variants.name: scaly_aggregate
habit_variants.trigger: low σ OR T<400°C — scaly/foliated aggregate (the etymology habit, from Greek lepidos 'scale')

lepidolite.description: K(Li,Al)₃(Al,Si)₄O₁₀(F,OH)₂ — monoclinic trioctahedral lithium mica, the polylithionite-trilithionite solid-solution series. Perfect {001} cleavage produces paper-thin flexible sheets (inelastic, unlike muscovite). The diagnostic pink-to-purple color comes from Mn²⁺ in the octahedral sites — Evans & Raftery 1982 settled this; trace lithium does not produce the color, despite folk-mineralogy claims. Late-pegmatite mineral: forms when residual fluid is enriched in Li + F + K simultaneously, AFTER spodumene + early micas have nucleated. Often replaces spodumene during late hydrothermal alteration (the documented Brazil/Maine paragenesis). Maximum size 20 cm books (Himalaya Mine, San Diego County); typical 1-10 mm scaly aggregates in vugs. THERMOLUMINESCENT — 50-200°C heating releases visible light from trapped defect charge carriers (no activator ion required). The Rožná pegmatite was where Bunsen & Kirchhoff extracted 150 kg of lepidolite in 1861 to isolate rubidium. Source: research/research-lepidolite.md (boss canonical 2026-05).

trace_ingredients.Mn: Mn²⁺ produces the diagnostic pink-to-purple chromophore (50-500 ppm) — the species signature
trace_ingredients.Fe: Fe²⁺/Fe³⁺ >500 ppm suppresses purple, shifts toward gray/greenish; >100 ppm chemically destabilizes lepidolite toward zinnwaldite
trace_ingredients.Rb: very late-stage K depletion concentrates Rb; produces rare rose-red Rb-rich variety (10% spawn rate)
trace_ingredients.Cs: trace substitution for K — pollucite paragenesis

lepidolite.thermal_decomp_reaction: Dehydroxylation begins >600°C; complete structural collapse >800°C → K-feldspar + corundum + Li-silicate melt + H₂O + HF

fluorescence.status: generally non-fluorescent under standard SW/MW/LW UV. NOTE: lepidolite IS thermoluminescent — heating to 50-200°C releases visible light from trapped charge carriers. Distinct mechanism from photo-fluorescence; flagged here for future render-hook (heat-stage thermal probe) — not a runtime mechanic in v86.

thermoluminescence.mechanism: trapped defect charge carriers released by thermal energy; no activator ion required
thermoluminescence.note: research/research-lepidolite.md §Fluorescence & Luminescence: "Some specimens show thermoluminescence — they emit visible light when heated to 50-200°C"

twin_laws.name: lepidolite_001_310
twin_laws.trigger: rare twin (per research-lepidolite.md §Twin Laws); pseudohexagonal composite from monoclinic basis

acid_dissolution.reaction: Slowly attacked by strong mineral acids; HF degrades silicate structure (uncommon condition). Otherwise highly resistant.

purple.trigger: Mn >= 2 ppm (any pegmatite background trace) — the diagnostic chromophore

purplish_pink.trigger: Mn 200-500 ppm — deeper purple, classic Cruzeiro book

rose_pink.trigger: very late-stage Rb-rich variety (rare); K depletion + Rb concentration

gray_black.trigger: Fe > 50 ppm — Fe²⁺/Fe³⁺ suppresses Mn chromophore, gray-greenish cast

pale_yellow.trigger: low Mn (<2 ppm) + low Fe (<50 ppm) — clean lepidolite, near-colorless to pale yellow

lepidolite.audit_status: new v86 (2026-05-19). Second Li-silicate (after spodumene); first F-mica species. Slot: gem_pegmatite Li phase (step 130+); the Cruzeiro paragenesis explicitly documents lepidolite per Cassedanne 1991. The thermoluminescence property is unique among shipped minerals and flagged for future render hook (heat-stage thermal probe activates emission). Mn-purple variety dispatch + spodumene-replacement substrate preference both per research-lepidolite.md (boss canonical 2026-05).
lepidolite._late_stage_note: Proposal C (2026-05): propensity:0.7. Lepidolite is a late-pegmatite mineral — forms in residual fluid AFTER early micas, feldspars, and tourmaline crystallize. The 0.7 propensity matches its position in the LCT pegmatite sequence (core/core-margin → late hydrothermal), comparable to chalcanthite/erythrite efflorescent crusts.

anglesite.description: PbSO₄ — orthorhombic, brilliant adamantine luster (one of the most lustrous non-metallic minerals). Intermediate step in the lead oxidation sequence: galena → anglesite → cerussite. Forms at the first wave of oxidation when sulfide-S becomes sulfate-S; transient in carbonate groundwater, where it dissolves and reprecipitates as cerussite. Named for Anglesey island (Wales) where the type specimens were found.

trace_ingredients.Fe: yellow to amber tint
trace_ingredients.Cu: pale blue-green tint

fluorescence.note: inconsistent, many specimens show no response

twin_laws.name: contact_110
twin_laws._source: Anthony Handbook v.V. Anglesite (orthorhombic Pnma, PbSO4) is the Pb endmember of the barite-group, isostructural with celestine. Same {110} contact twins documented at Monteponi (Sardinia) and Sierra de Cordoba (Argentina) supergene oxidation specimens. Conservative p=0.02 matching celestine.

acid_dissolution.reaction: PbSO₄ + 2H⁺ → Pb²⁺ + HSO₄⁻ (slow, only strong acid)

anglesite.audit_status: new (supergene round 1, lead oxidation sequence). Not in agent-api/vugg-agent.js.

cerussite.description: PbCO₃ — orthorhombic lead carbonate. Water-clear with adamantine luster and extreme birefringence (a thin slice shows doubled images). Final stable product of the lead oxidation sequence in carbonate-rich environments: galena → anglesite → cerussite. Stellate six-ray twin (cyclic twin on {110}) is among mineralogy's most iconic forms — three individuals intergrown at 120° angles producing a perfect star. Named for Latin 'cerussa' (white lead).

trace_ingredients.Cu: blue-green aurichalcite-adjacent tint

acid_dissolution.reaction: PbCO₃ + 2H⁺ → Pb²⁺ + H₂O + CO₂ (fizzes)

cerussite.audit_status: new (supergene round 1, lead oxidation sequence). Classic cyclic twins. Not in agent-api/vugg-agent.js.

pyromorphite.description: Pb₅(PO₄)₃Cl — hexagonal apatite-group phosphate. Barrel-shaped prisms in olive-green, yellow-green, brown, or orange. Forms in supergene oxidation zones when phosphate-bearing meteoric water meets existing Pb minerals — galena, anglesite, cerussite can all be pseudomorphed. Apatite-group solid-solution end-member: pyromorphite–mimetite–vanadinite is one of mineralogy's most complete compositional series. Low toxicity (stable Pb) — pyromorphite is used in environmental remediation of Pb-contaminated soils by feeding the soil phosphate.

twin_laws.name: contact_1122
twin_laws._source: Anthony Handbook v.IV (phosphates) + Frondel 1958 apatite-group section. Pyromorphite (hexagonal P6₃/m, apatite-group) rarely twins — apatite-group structures have low intrinsic twin propensity. Sparse {11-22} or {10-11} contact twins are reported at Bad Ems (Germany) and Leadhills (Scotland). Conservative p=0.005 (rare-twin floor).

acid_dissolution.reaction: Pb₅(PO₄)₃Cl + 10H⁺ → 5Pb²⁺ + 3H₃PO₄ + Cl⁻

pyromorphite.audit_status: new (supergene round 1). First P consumer in the sim. Apatite-group cousin of existing mimetite + new vanadinite. Not in agent-api/vugg-agent.js.

vanadinite.description: Pb₅(VO₄)₃Cl — hexagonal apatite-group vanadate. Bright red-orange to brown-orange hexagonal prisms, one of the most recognizable minerals in a collector case. Vanadate end-member of the pyromorphite-mimetite-vanadinite series. Vanadium comes from oxidation of V-bearing sediments (red-bed ironstones, roll-fronts) in arid climates — the ACF mines of Morocco and the Arizona desert have produced the finest specimens. Classified here under phosphate (apatite-group structure); the 12-hue palette has no vanadate class so we fold with P/As/V together.

twin_laws.name: contact_1122
twin_laws._source: Anthony Handbook v.IV + Mindat vanadinite habit notes. Isostructural with pyromorphite (apatite-group, hexagonal P6₃/m, V endmember). Same low twin propensity; rare {11-22} contact twins documented at Mibladen (Morocco) and Old Yuma Mine (Arizona). Conservative p=0.005 floor.

acid_dissolution.reaction: Pb₅(VO₄)₃Cl + 10H⁺ → 5Pb²⁺ + 3H₃VO₄ + Cl⁻

vanadinite.audit_status: new (supergene round 1). First V consumer in the sim. Apatite-group cousin of pyromorphite + mimetite. Folded into phosphate class (no vanadate hue in 12-wheel). Not in agent-api/vugg-agent.js.

bornite.description: Cu₅FeS₄ — the 228°C order-disorder transition mineral. Above 228°C, Cu and Fe randomly occupy the cation sites (pseudo-cubic, high-T form); below, they order (orthorhombic). Fresh bronze-copper color on a broken surface, but the mineral tarnishes rapidly to the iridescent blue/purple/green/gold 'peacock ore' from thin-film interference on sulfide surface products. Forms both hypogene (hydrothermal, ~200–500°C) and supergene (cooler, as an enrichment mineral replacing chalcopyrite). Competes with chalcopyrite for Cu+Fe+S at different Cu:Fe ratios.

twin_laws.name: inversion_111
twin_laws._source: Ramdohr 1980 §4.3 sulfide section. Bornite undergoes a cubic→orthorhombic ordering transition at 228°C; the inversion produces {111} polysynthetic exsolution lamellae that are commonly visible in reflected light. Field frequency in supergene/hypogene specimens ~5-15%; conservative p=0.03 to minimize cascade for the sample batch.

acid_dissolution.reaction: Cu₅FeS₄ + O₂ + acid → Cu²⁺ + Fe³⁺ + S (supergene oxidation)

bornite.audit_status: new (supergene round 2, Cu-sulfide suite). Competes with chalcopyrite. Not in agent-api/vugg-agent.js.

chalcocite.description: Cu₂S — 79.8% Cu by weight, one of the richest copper ores. The pseudomorph thief: replaces chalcopyrite and bornite atom-by-atom in the supergene enrichment blanket, keeping the host's outline while swapping the chemistry. Forms dark gray to black metallic crystals. Pseudo-hexagonal stellate twins (cyclic sixlings on {110}) are the collector's prize. The supergene enrichment zone is where mineable copper ore gets made — weathering concentrates Cu into a layer richer than the primary sulfide above it.

trace_ingredients.Fe: trace — chalcocite→bornite series

chalcocite.thermal_decomp_reaction: Cu₂S (high-T cubic) ↔ Cu₂S (low-T orthorhombic) phase transition at 103°C

covellite.description: CuS — indigo-blue, the only common naturally blue mineral. Micaceous perfect basal cleavage (peels into flexible sheets like mica). Higher S:Cu ratio than chalcocite (1:1 vs 1:2), so it forms where sulfide activity is higher — at the boundary between the supergene oxidation zone and the reduced primary sulfides below. Decomposes to chalcocite + sulfur at 507°C. The name is Latin 'covellite' (for Niccolo Covelli, who discovered the Vesuvius fumarole specimens in 1833).

trace_ingredients.Fe: subtle bronze-sheen shift

twin_laws.name: cleavage_twin_1122
twin_laws._source: Anthony Handbook v.I (CuS section) + Ramdohr 1980 §4.3 copper-sulfide section. Covellite (hexagonal P6₃/mmc, micaceous basal cleavage) has rare {11-22} contact twins documented at Butte and Calabona. Most covellite occurs as iridescent plates without visible twins because the basal cleavage dominates the surface morphology. p=0.005 (rare-twin floor with data-sparse note).

acid_dissolution.reaction: CuS + 2H⁺ + ½O₂ → Cu²⁺ + H₂O + S

chrysocolla.description: Cu₂H₂Si₂O₅(OH)₄, the cyan-blue copper silicate. Cryptocrystalline and X-ray amorphous — technically a mineraloid, though sanctioned as a mineral since 1803. Forms at 5–60 °C where meteoric groundwater carries Cu²⁺ out of a weathering copper deposit and picks up SiO₂ from silicate wall rock. At Bisbee it crusts over cuprite and native copper and famously pseudomorphs azurite once pCO₂ drops and silica floods in.

trace_ingredients.Fe: limonite contamination — brown shift

chrysocolla.thermal_decomp_reaction: chrysocolla dehydrates above ~100 °C, tending toward plancheite/shattuckite

Per Anthony Handbook v.II and Mindat chrysocolla habit notes. v136 silicate batch documented this explicitly rather than padding with placeholder p=0.005.

acid_dissolution.reaction: chrysocolla dissolves in strong acid — Cu²⁺ + silicic acid released

chrysocolla.audit_status: new — Bisbee scenario centrepiece, azurite-pseudomorph pCO₂-drop mechanic
chrysocolla._late_stage_note: Proposal C (2026-05): propensity:0.6. Botryoidal cyan crust on copper minerals; late supergene final cover at Bisbee, Tsumeb, Chuquicamata.

cuprite.description: Cu₂O — 88.8% Cu by weight. Forms at the Eh boundary between native copper (more reducing) and tenorite/malachite (more oxidizing): a narrow redox window controls its existence. Dark red to nearly black with ruby-red internal reflections (from refraction, not fluorescence). Classic habits include octahedral crystals, 'chalcotrichite' hair-like plush, and massive 'tile ore'. Often occurs with native copper and malachite in supergene Cu deposits.

trace_ingredients.Fe: variant iron red

acid_dissolution.reaction: Cu₂O + 2H⁺ → 2Cu²⁺ + H₂O

cuprite.audit_status: new (supergene round 3, Cu oxide/native suite). Forms at the narrow Eh window between reducing and oxidizing. Not in agent-api/vugg-agent.js.

azurite.description: Cu₃(CO₃)₂(OH)₂ — deep azure to midnight blue, the deepest blue in the common mineral kingdom. Forms in supergene oxidation zones where high pCO₂ groundwater meets Cu²⁺. Most azurite is born to become malachite: CO₂ escapes from solution over time and the higher-carbonate azurite converts to lower-carbonate malachite, preserving the original crystal shape as a green pseudomorph. Requires carbonate country rock (limestone host) for the high pCO₂ conditions.

twin_laws.name: contact_001
twin_laws._source: Anthony Handbook v.V + Frondel 1962 v.III — Frondel's azurite section gives the most thorough twin documentation of any Cu-carbonate. Azurite (monoclinic P21/c, Cu3(CO3)2(OH)2) shows {001} contact twins commonly at Tsumeb, Bisbee, and Chessy-les-Mines (France, type locality). The "deep blue prismatic" habit is famously twin-prone — collector-grade Bisbee azurite from the Copper Queen routinely shows contact-twin V-pairs. p=0.05 (minor common) reflects the relatively high visible-twin frequency.

acid_dissolution.reaction: Cu₃(CO₃)₂(OH)₂ + 6H⁺ → 3Cu²⁺ + 2H₂O + 2CO₂ (fizzes like calcite)

azurite.audit_status: new (supergene round 3). Azurite→malachite conversion mechanic implemented via CO3 depletion check in grow_azurite. Not in agent-api/vugg-agent.js.
azurite._late_stage_note: Proposal C (2026-05): propensity:0.3. Late Cu carbonate; partners with malachite but often crystallizes after primary Cu phases.

native_copper.description: Cu — elemental native copper. Only forms when S²⁻ is absent (otherwise precipitates as sulfide) AND Eh is strongly reducing (otherwise oxidizes to cuprite/malachite). The Michigan Keweenaw peninsula basalt vesicles produced 500-ton masses from basalt-hosted brines. Copper-red fresh, tarnishes brown, eventually green (malachite coating). The Statue of Liberty's iconic patina IS malachite growing on native copper — the reverse of supergene ore formation, where rain and air turn metal into mineral in front of us.
native_copper.thermal_decomp_reaction: Cu melts at 1083°C (mineral itself stable well below)

acid_dissolution.reaction: Cu + oxidizing acid → Cu²⁺ (slow)

native_copper.audit_status: new (supergene round 3). First native-element mineral. Shares native-hue class_color with future native bismuth. Not in agent-api/vugg-agent.js.

native_gold.description: Au — elemental native gold, the noble metal. Two precipitation pathways the sim collapses into one supersaturation function: (1) high-T magmatic-hydrothermal Au-Cl complex destabilization at boiling/decompression (Bingham vapor-plume mechanism per Landtwing et al. 2010), and (2) low-T supergene Au-Cl reduction at the redox interface, often coupled with chalcocite enrichment (Bisbee oxidation-cap mechanism per Graeme et al. 2019). Tolerates both oxidizing AND reducing fluids because the two transport complexes (Au-Cl and Au-HS) cover both Eh regimes. Crystal habits range from octahedral {111} (slow growth, rare) through dendritic / spongy (the diagnostic supergene Au) to massive nuggets (rapid precipitation in pockets). Color goes from rich yellow at pure Au through pale yellow electrum (>10% Ag alloy) to rose-gold cuproauride affinity (Cu-rich systems like Bisbee). Essentially indestructible in surface conditions — no acid-dissolution path in the engine.

trace_ingredients.Cu: Cu-Au cuproauride / rose-gold affinity (Cu-rich systems)
trace_ingredients.Te: Au-Te telluride competition (calaverite/sylvanite if Te + S available — future engine)

native_gold.thermal_decomp_reaction: Au melts at 1064°C; mineral itself stable at all sim T (no thermal decomposition path)

pale_yellow_electrum.trigger: Ag-alloy (Ag > 5 ppm and Ag dominant over Cu)

native_gold.audit_status: new (Au field + grow_native_gold landed via Bingham/Bisbee pending_schema_additions promotion). Activates Bingham Au=2 and Bisbee Au=3 fluid values that were pre-researched during the chemistry audit. Not in agent-api/vugg-agent.js.

magnetite.description: Fe₃O₄ — mixed-valence Fe²⁺Fe³⁺₂O₄ spinel oxide. Black, metallic-to-submetallic, strongly magnetic (lodestone is natural permanent-magnet magnetite). Forms at the hematite-magnetite (HM) redox buffer — one of geochemistry's most important reference frames. Crossing the HM boundary transforms the entire mineral assemblage. Octahedral {111} and rhombic dodecahedral {110} are the classic habits; martite pseudomorph is magnetite after oxidation to hematite at the crystal surface. Streak: black (diagnostic against hematite's red streak).
magnetite.thermal_decomp_reaction: Fe₃O₄ oxidizes to Fe₂O₃ above 800°C in air (martite)

acid_dissolution.reaction: Fe₃O₄ + 8H⁺ → Fe²⁺ + 2Fe³⁺ + 4H₂O (slow, oxidative)

magnetite.audit_status: new (round 4, iron + Sb-Bi + misc). Mixed-valence Fe²⁺Fe³⁺ oxide at the HM redox buffer. Not in agent-api/vugg-agent.js.

lepidocrocite.description: γ-FeOOH — the ruby-red hydroxide dimorph of goethite. Same formula, different crystal structure: goethite is a 3D framework (yellow-brown needles), lepidocrocite is layered (ruby-red micaceous plates). Particle size controls the hue — nanoscale plates appear pink to mauve, coarser crystals rust-red. Kinetically favored over goethite under RAPID oxidation of Fe²⁺ (e.g., pyrite weathering in situ); slow oxidation gives goethite instead. Transforms to goethite over geological time or to maghemite/hematite when heated. 'Lithium quartz' sold in rock shops is actually nanoscale lepidocrocite included in quartz.

twin_laws.name: contact_100
twin_laws._source: Anthony Handbook v.V + Mindat lepidocrocite habit notes. Lepidocrocite (orthorhombic Cmcm, gamma-FeO(OH)) forms ruby-red micaceous platy scales at Easton (PA) + Siegen (Germany) + Cornwall. Rare {100} contact twins reported but data sparse — platy aggregates dominate visible morphology. p=0.005 (rare-twin floor with data-sparse note).

acid_dissolution.reaction: γ-FeOOH + 3H⁺ → Fe³⁺ + 2H₂O

stibnite.description: Sb₂S₃ — orthorhombic antimony sulfide, lead-gray to steel-gray with brilliant metallic luster on fresh cleavage. One of the most visually striking minerals: sword-like elongated blades up to 60 cm at Ichinokawa (Japan). Same structure as bismuthinite (Bi₂S₃) — Sb and Bi are geochemical cousins. Low-melting (550°C), so stibnite is destroyed by minor metamorphism. Primary Sb ore. Dendritic growth when rapidly deposited; single sword-blades when slow.

twin_laws.name: contact_130
twin_laws._source: Dana 8th ed. + Anthony Handbook v.I + Ramdohr 1980 §4.6 antimony-sulfide section. Stibnite (orthorhombic Pbnm) shows contact + cyclic twins on {130}, documented at Ichinokawa (Japan) and Felsőbánya (Romania) where the bladed habit occasionally produces V-pair contact twins. Conservative p=0.02 — most acicular stibnite is untwinned.

acid_dissolution.reaction: Sb₂S₃ + 6H⁺ → 2Sb³⁺ + 3H₂S

bismuthinite.description: Bi₂S₃ — orthorhombic bismuth sulfide, same structure as stibnite. Lead-gray to tin-white metallic, often showing yellowish/iridescent tarnish. Forms in high-T hydrothermal veins associated with arsenopyrite, chalcopyrite, cassiterite, wolframite — the tin-tungsten-arsenic greisen suite. Temperature-driven habit switch: acicular needles at T < 350°C, stout prisms at T > 350°C. When S²⁻ runs out before Bi, switches to native bismuth.

twin_laws.name: contact_130
twin_laws._source: Anthony Handbook v.I + Ramdohr 1980 §4.6. Isostructural with stibnite (orthorhombic Pbnm). Twinning behavior parallels stibnite; conservative p=0.02 reflects the same rare-but-documented field frequency.

acid_dissolution.reaction: Bi₂S₃ + 6H⁺ → 2Bi³⁺ + 3H₂S

native_bismuth.description: Bi — elemental native bismuth. Silver-white on fresh surface, iridescent oxide tarnish (yellow, blue, pink) develops rapidly. Melts at an unusually low 271.5°C — the lowest of any native metal. Forms only when sulfur runs out before bismuth does — bismuthinite uses the available S first, then residual Bi crystallizes native. The rainbow 'hoppered' Bi crystals sold in rock shops are lab-grown, not natural; natural native bismuth is typically arborescent/massive/rarely rhombohedral.
native_bismuth.thermal_decomp_reaction: Bi melts at 271.5°C — the lowest melting native metal

twin_laws.name: contact_0112
twin_laws._source: Anthony Handbook v.I (native elements). Native bismuth (trigonal R-3m, Bi) shows {01-12} contact + {01-14} rotation twins in well-formed rhombohedral platelets at Schneeberg (Saxony) + Cobalt (Ontario) five-element-vein specimens. Most native bismuth occurs as arborescent / skeletal aggregates of small platelets — the arborescent habit is the cluster pattern, not the individual crystal; per-platelet twinning is rare but documented. Conservative p=0.005 (rare-twin floor reflecting the per-platelet probability).

acid_dissolution.reaction: Bi + oxidizing acid → Bi³⁺

clinobisvanite.description: BiVO₄ — bright yellow to orange-yellow monoclinic bismuth vanadate. End of the bismuth oxidation sequence: bismuthinite → native bismuth → bismite/bismutite → clinobisvanite (if V is present). Microscopic — individual crystals rarely exceed 0.1 mm, so clinobisvanite appears as a powdery to micro-platy coating on matrix. Famously, BiVO₄ is a photocatalyst for water splitting — the same mineral that forms as a supergene afterthought is being engineered to make solar fuel. Folded into phosphate class_color (#13eb7f) per the 12-hue palette decision.

twin_laws.name: contact_110
twin_laws._source: Anthony Handbook v.IV + Mindat clinobisvanite (BiVO₄, monoclinic I2/a) habit notes. Data sparse — clinobisvanite typically occurs as fine yellow micro-plates at supergene Bi-Cu vein zones (Hingston Down Quarry, Sardinian Mt. Cobre). Rare twinning reported but not well-characterized; conservative p=0.005 (rare-twin floor with data-sparse note).

acid_dissolution.reaction: BiVO₄ + 4H⁺ → Bi³⁺ + VO₂⁺ + 2H₂O

emerald.description: Be₃Al₂Si₆O₁₈ with Cr³⁺ (or V³⁺) substituting for Al — the green variety of beryl. The 'emerald paradox': Cr is ultramafic, Be is pegmatitic; these chemistries almost never coexist. Emerald forms where pegmatite fluid meets ultramafic country rock (Colombia Muzo shale-hosted, Zambia Kagem schist-hosted, Cruzeiro pegmatite-only). Top Colombian gem emerald is rarer than diamond as gem material.

trace_ingredients.V: alternative chromophore (V³⁺, dominant in Colombian emerald)
trace_ingredients.Fe: trace; present in all natural emeralds, not a chromophore at low levels

emerald.fluorescence: weak red (Cr³⁺ emission, overlaps ruby but much weaker)

twin_laws.name: rare_0001
twin_laws._source: Same beryl-group basal {0001} twin as the parent beryl entry — emerald is the Cr-bearing color variant of Be3Al2Si6O18, inherits the same rare-twin behavior. Frondel 1962 v.III; Anthony Handbook v.II.

aquamarine.description: Be₃Al₂Si₆O₁₈ with Fe²⁺ — the blue variety of beryl. Most abundant gem beryl variety; every gem-producing pegmatite yields aquamarine. Santa Maria (Pakistan Shigar) is the deep-blue benchmark; Cruzeiro and Padre Paraíso (Brazil Minas Gerais) are the volume producers. Fires when Fe ≥ 8 ppm in the Fe²⁺ state (non-oxidizing).

trace_ingredients.Fe: principal chromophore (Fe²⁺, channel + Al sites)

twin_laws.name: rare_0001

morganite.description: Be₃Al₂Si₆O₁₈ with Mn²⁺ — the pink-to-peach variety of beryl. Mn²⁺ sits in the Al octahedral site; natural irradiation over geologic time partially oxidizes it to Mn³⁺, producing the pink hue. Named by George F. Kunz of Tiffany & Co (1911) for J.P. Morgan. Late-stage pegmatite mineral — Mn accumulates in fluid while earlier phases (feldspar, quartz, aquamarine) crystallize. Pala District CA, Madagascar, Minas Gerais are top sources.

trace_ingredients.Cs: minor — red-beryl/bixbite is Mn³⁺+Cs, extremely rare

twin_laws.name: rare_0001
twin_laws._source: Same beryl-group basal {0001} twin as the parent beryl entry — morganite is the Mn/Cs-bearing pink color variant of Be3Al2Si6O18, inherits the same rare-twin behavior.

pink_morganite.trigger: Mn²⁺/Mn³⁺ mixed (irradiated), the standard collector habit

morganite.audit_status: Round 7 Option-A split. Fires when Mn ≥ 2.0 with no Cr/V above emerald gate (Cr < 0.5, V < 1.0). Takes precedence over heliodor/aquamarine in the beryl-family chromophore dispatch.

heliodor.description: Be₃Al₂Si₆O₁₈ with Fe³⁺ (oxidized) — the yellow variety of beryl. Same Fe cation as aquamarine but in the 3+ oxidation state; the aquamarine/heliodor split is the cleanest redox record in the gem world. Volodarsk (Namibia) is the type locality for deep-yellow 'Namibian heliodor'; Urals (Russia) and Minas Gerais (Brazil) also produce.

trace_ingredients.Fe: principal chromophore (Fe³⁺, oxidized state)

heliodor.audit_status: Round 7 Option-A split. Fires when Fe ≥ 15 AND O2 > 0.5 with no higher-priority chromophore (Cr/V/Mn). Takes precedence over aquamarine when Fe is both high and oxidized.

corundum.description: Al₂O₃ — trigonal close-packed oxide. The structural parent of ruby + sapphire, and the hardness-9 abrasive mineral (emery is Al₂O₃ + magnetite). Corundum is SiO₂-undersaturated: if any significant silica is present, Al + SiO₂ drives toward the Al₂SiO₅ polymorphs (kyanite/andalusite/sillimanite) or feldspar, not corundum. The defining constraint is SiO₂ < 50 ppm — the first upper-bound gate in this simulator. Forms in amphibolite-to-granulite-facies marble contact metamorphism (Mogok-type) and in basalt-hosted xenocrysts.

trace_ingredients.Fe: pale brown (below ruby gate)
trace_ingredients.Cr: trace colorless at <2 ppm (above becomes ruby)

corundum.audit_status: Round 7 Commit 3. First upper-bound-gated mineral in the sim: SiO2 < 50 blocks nucleation. Anchored to Mogok marble contact metamorphism scenario via gate + scenarios entry.

ruby._retune_note_nucleation_sigma: Stale-mineral retune (2026-05): dropped 1.5 → 1.3 to match the actual σ ceiling produced by supersaturation_ruby() under marble_contact_metamorphism conditions. tools/stale_mineral_probe.mjs sweep showed σ peaked at 1.42 in 120/540 steps but never crossed the prior 1.5 threshold — engine had a structural mismatch with the formula's `base * min(Cr/5, 2)` ceiling (formula doesn't reach 1.5 unless Cr ≥ 10 ppm AND base ≥ 1.5, neither typical of marble fluids). Corundum-family priority preserved: ruby > sapphire > corundum, enforced by array order in 89-nucleation-silicate.ts rather than threshold spacing.

ruby.description: Al₂O₃ + Cr³⁺ — the red chromium-bearing variety of corundum. Cr substitutes for Al at the 100-5000 ppm level in the Al octahedral site; the d-d transitions absorb blue-green and transmit red, plus fluoresce strongly red under UV (the 'ruby laser' physics). Top gem 'pigeon's blood' ruby from Mogok Burma shows medium-dark red with blue undertone from trace Fe + strong Cr fluorescence. Requires SiO₂ undersaturation (shared corundum constraint) AND Cr >= 2 ppm. Below Cr=2 the same corundum crystal would be pink sapphire or colorless.

trace_ingredients.Fe: trace — modulates fluorescence (low Fe = strong red fluorescence, Mogok signature)

ruby.fluorescence: strong red under LW + SW UV (Cr³⁺ emission at 694 nm) — diagnostic

pigeons_blood.trigger: Cr + trace Fe, strong fluorescence (Mogok signature)

ruby.audit_status: Round 7 Commit 3. Shares the SiO2 < 50 upper gate with corundum family. Priority over pink-sapphire + colorless-corundum when Cr >= 2 ppm.

habit_variants.name: barrel
habit_variants.trigger: default basalt-hosted — steep dipyramid, Montana Yogo habit

habit_variants.name: tabular
habit_variants.trigger: marble-hosted — flat hex plate

habit_variants.trigger: intermediate σ — short hex prism

habit_variants.name: asterated
habit_variants.trigger: rutile-inclusion trigger — 6-rayed or 12-rayed star sapphire

sapphire.description: Al₂O₃ with Fe + Ti (blue), Fe alone (yellow), V (violet), or trace Cr (pink-orange padparadscha) — the non-red corundum varieties. Blue sapphire's color is the Fe²⁺-Ti⁴⁺ intervalence charge transfer (IVCT), a well-characterized gem mechanism requiring both cations at the right concentration. Kashmir 'cornflower blue' is the classic; Montana Yogo, Sri Lanka, Madagascar, Thailand/Cambodia are active sources. Shares the SiO₂ < 50 upper gate with corundum + ruby.

trace_ingredients.Fe: principal blue/yellow chromophore (Fe³⁺ or Fe²⁺-Ti⁴⁺ IVCT)
trace_ingredients.Ti: blue sapphire partner (Fe+Ti intervalence charge transfer)
trace_ingredients.V: violet sapphire chromophore (Tanzania) at higher concentration

sapphire.fluorescence: weak — Fe quenches Cr/V emission; yellow sapphire shows weak orange (Fe³⁺)

cornflower_kashmir.trigger: low Fe + perfect Ti balance; microscale silk scatters light

yellow.trigger: Fe³⁺ alone (Fe >= 20, no Ti) — Sri Lanka, Montana

green.trigger: Fe alone no Ti, specific oxidation — Australia, Montana

padparadscha.trigger: Cr + trace Fe — pink-orange, Sri Lanka + Madagascar

color_change.trigger: Cr + V + Fe combination — Umba Valley Tanzania

sapphire.audit_status: Round 7 Commit 3. Multi-color dispatch: blue (Fe+Ti), yellow (Fe alone ≥ 20), pink (Cr < 2), padparadscha (Cr + trace Fe), violet (V ≥ 2). Shares SiO2 < 50 upper gate with corundum/ruby.

habit_variants.name: massive_granular
habit_variants.trigger: low σ — granular vein filling, the dominant economic form of Ag₂S

habit_variants.name: prismatic
habit_variants.trigger: mid σ — primary low-T elongated prismatic growth

habit_variants.name: thorn
habit_variants.trigger: high σ — spiky prismatic projections, the species' diagnostic (ακανθα = thorn)

habit_variants.name: pseudo_cubic
habit_variants.trigger: paramorph — set by apply_paramorph_transitions when an argentite crystal cools below 173°C; retains the cubic external form of its high-T parent

acanthite.description: Ag₂S — monoclinic silver sulfide and the most important silver ore on Earth at 87% Ag by weight. Lead-gray to iron-black metallic, Mohs 2-2.5, sectile (cuts like lead). The cold-storage form: above 173°C the same composition crystallizes as cubic argentite, and most acanthite in nature is paramorphic after cooled argentite, retaining the cubic external form while the lattice underneath inverts to monoclinic (Petruk et al. 1974, Canadian Mineralogist 12). Forms in low-sulfidation epithermal veins, mesothermal silver veins, and supergene enrichment zones; classic localities include Guanajuato (Mexico, pseudo-cubic crystals), Freiberg (Saxony), and Kongsberg (Norway).

twin_laws.name: paramorphic_111
twin_laws._source: Ramdohr 1980 §4.7 silver-sulfide section. Acanthite (monoclinic P2₁/n, stable below 177°C) commonly inherits paramorphic {111} twin geometry from the high-T cubic argentite phase (Ag₂S inverts on cooling). At Freiberg / Kongsberg, acanthite preserves the cubic-habit external shape with internal paramorphic twins. p=0.02 reflects rare visible-twin frequency — most acanthite is massive or wire-form.

acanthite.audit_status: Round 8a-1 (Apr 2026). Engine + supersaturation + nucleation + narrator complete in vugg.py + web/index.html; JSON entry mirrored to docs/. Habit variants: massive_granular (low σ, dominant economic form), prismatic (mid σ), thorn (high σ), pseudo_cubic (paramorph hook — set in 8a-2 when argentite cools through 173°C). Sources: Petruk et al. 1974 (Canadian Mineralogist 12: 365-369), Hayba & Bethke 1985 (Reviews in Economic Geology 2). Activates dormant Ag pool at MVT/Sweetwater/Tsumeb/Bisbee. First Ag mineral in the sim.

habit_variants.name: cubic
habit_variants.trigger: low-moderate σ — sharp {100} cubes, the Comstock Lode high-T habit

habit_variants.name: octahedral
habit_variants.trigger: moderate σ — {111} octahedra, growth-rate-dependent; spinel-law penetration twins

habit_variants.name: arborescent
habit_variants.trigger: high σ — dendritic / wire-like aggregates in epithermal vein settings

argentite.description: Ag₂S — body-centered cubic silver sulfide, the high-temperature polymorph stable only above 173°C. A ghost mineral: every argentite specimen at room temperature is actually acanthite (monoclinic) wearing argentite's cubic crystal habit, because below 173°C the lattice inverts in place while the external form stays frozen — handled by the apply_paramorph_transitions hook in vugg.py. Lead-gray to black metallic, Mohs 2-2.5, sectile. Forms in the high-T zones of epithermal and mesothermal Ag-bearing veins; classic localities include Comstock Lode (Nevada), Pachuca-Real del Monte (Mexico), and Freiberg (Saxony). Source: research/research-argentite.md (boss commit f2939da), Petruk et al. 1974.

twin_laws.name: spinel_111
twin_laws.trigger: growth — spinel-law penetration twin, less common than the cooled-acanthite paramorph form

argentite.audit_status: Round 8a-2 (Apr 2026). Engine + supersaturation + nucleation + narrator complete in vugg.py + web/index.html. Hard lower-T gate at 173°C; below that, apply_paramorph_transitions hook converts the crystal in-place to acanthite while preserving habit + dominant_forms — first non-destructive polymorph mechanic in the sim, distinct from THERMAL_DECOMPOSITION which destroys the crystal. Habit variants: cubic (low-moderate σ, Comstock Lode signature), octahedral (mid σ, with {111} spinel-law penetration twins), arborescent (high σ, dendritic/wire-like). Activates dormant Ag pool only in scenarios that reach >173°C — currently radioactive_pegmatite per declared scenarios. Sources: Petruk et al. 1974 (Canadian Mineralogist 12), boss research file research/research-argentite.md.

habit_variants.name: wire
habit_variants.trigger: low T + high σ — Kongsberg epithermal wire habit, the collector's prize; threads of metal curling through open vug space

habit_variants.name: dendritic
habit_variants.trigger: moderate σ — Cobalt-Ontario fern-plate habit; branching pattern from diffusion-limited growth

habit_variants.name: massive
habit_variants.trigger: low σ — Keweenaw nugget habit, hackly metallic mass; high-Ag concentration depletes locally before delicate morphologies develop

habit_variants.name: cubic_crystal
habit_variants.trigger: high T (>200°C) + low σ — rare hypogene primary cubic crystal; {100} faces with {111} modifier; can have {111} penetration twin

native_silver.description: Ag — elemental silver, the only native element bright enough to make you rich just by looking at it wrong. Cubic isometric (Fm3̄m), Mohs 2.5-3, specific gravity 10.5 (one of the heaviest native metals). The S-depletion mineral: native silver only forms where every sulfur atom is already claimed and the fluid is strongly reducing — the inverse of normal supersaturation logic. Famous wire-silver localities (Kongsberg Norway, Keweenaw Michigan, Cobalt Ontario) all sit in S-depleted reducing pockets. Tarnishes inevitably to a dark acanthite rind from atmospheric H₂S; freshly broken surfaces still flash with the brightest metallic luster known to mineralogy. Source: research/research-native-silver.md (boss commit f2939da), Boyle 1968 (GSA Bulletin 79), Bjørlykke 1959 (NGU 207).

twin_laws.name: 111_penetration
twin_laws.trigger: growth at specific localities (Batopilas Mexico, Chanarcillo Chile, Kongsberg Norway) — spinel-law penetration twins, locality-driven not universal

native_silver.audit_status: Round 8a-3 (Apr 2026). Engine + supersaturation + nucleation + narrator complete in vugg.py + web/index.html; JSON entry mirrored to docs/. The first depletion-gate engine in the sim: native silver fires only when fluid.S < 2 ppm AND fluid.O2 < 0.3 — the geological signature of every Kongsberg-type wire-silver locality. Engine includes tarnish branch (when S returns to fluid > 5 ppm, the surface skins over with acanthite as a dissolution event). Habit variants: wire (low T + high σ, Kongsberg), dendritic (mid σ, Cobalt-Ontario), massive (low σ, Keweenaw), cubic_crystal (rare hypogene primary with {111} penetration twin). Activates the dormant Ag pool at Tsumeb supergene + Bisbee oxidation cap, where dissolving acanthite/tetrahedrite releases Ag⁺ that meets a deeper reducing pocket. Tarnish-clock mechanic (per-step acanthite-rind accumulation regardless of S availability) is deferred to 8a-3b alongside the same mechanic for native_arsenic / native_tellurium / native_bismuth. Sources: Boyle 1968 (GSA Bulletin 79), Bjørlykke 1959 (NGU 207), Kissin & Mango 2014 (CIM SV 54), boss research file research/research-native-silver.md.

habit_variants.name: massive_granular
habit_variants.trigger: low-mid σ — Freiberg ore form, granular crusts; tin-white metallic mass; the dominant economic habit

habit_variants.name: reniform
habit_variants.trigger: high σ — botryoidal kidney-shaped crusts with concentric layers, the Akatani Mine habit

habit_variants.name: rhombohedral_crystal
habit_variants.trigger: high T (>250°C) + low σ — RARE rhombohedral crystals with {0001} basal pinacoid + {1011} faces

habit_variants.name: arsenolamprite
habit_variants.trigger: Bi-rich fluid (Bi>5) + mid σ — the Bi-substituted variety, possibly orthorhombic; up to 12% Bi

native_arsenic.description: As — elemental arsenic, the residual-overflow native element. Trigonal (R3̄m), Mohs 3-4, perfect basal {0001} cleavage that splits like dark mica. Tin-white to steel-gray metallic on fresh fracture, tarnishes to arsenolite (As₂O₃) within hours. Forms only when every other As-consumer in the fluid (Fe → arsenopyrite, Ni → nickeline, Co → safflorite, S → realgar/orpiment) has had its share — the leftover phase. Famous Co-Ni-Ag vein districts (Freiberg Saxony, Sainte-Marie-aux-Mines Alsace, Příbram Czech) host native arsenic as the residual after the Co/Ni/Ag captured the metals and S was already locked in. Sources: research/research-native-arsenic.md (boss commit f2939da), Schiferl 1969 (Acta Cryst. B25), Petruk 1971 (Cobalt-Ag paragenesis).
native_arsenic.thermal_decomp_reaction: As (s) → As (vapor) — sublimes at 615°C and 1 atm without melting
native_arsenic._twin_laws_note: Intentionally empty — native arsenic (trigonal R-3m, As) habit code 'massive_granular' signals non-euhedral aggregate. Almost always occurs as botryoidal + reniform + sooty masses at Schneeberg + Sankt Andreasberg + Pribram, not as individual euhedra. Anthony Handbook v.I notes native arsenic almost never forms euhedral crystals; the rare crystalline form (Schneeberg pillow specimens) is rhombohedral but the simulator renders the dominant massive habit. v141 final batch.
native_arsenic.audit_status: Round 8b-1 (Apr 2026). Engine + supersaturation + nucleation + narrator complete in vugg.py + web/index.html; JSON entry mirrored to docs/. The residual-overflow native element: hard gates at S>10 (overflows to realgar/orpiment/arsenopyrite), Fe>50 (overflows to arsenopyrite), O₂>0.5 (overflows to arsenate suite). Habit variants: massive_granular (Freiberg ore form), reniform (Akatani botryoidal), rhombohedral_crystal (rare high-T crystals), arsenolamprite (Bi-rich variety). Engine includes oxidative-dissolution branch when O₂>0.7 (As goes back to fluid as AsO₄³⁻, feeds the supergene arsenate cascade: scorodite/erythrite/annabergite/olivenite/mimetite). Tarnish-clock for the As₂O₃ surface bloom is deferred to 8a-3b alongside other native-element tarnish mechanics. Sources: Schiferl 1969 (Acta Cryst. B25), Petruk 1971 (Cobalt-Ag paragenesis), boss research file research/research-native-arsenic.md.

habit_variants.name: bipyramidal_alpha
habit_variants.trigger: T<95°C + low-mid σ — α-sulfur orthorhombic, the Sicilian Agrigento bipyramids; {111} steep + {113} shallow dipyramids on {001} pinacoid

habit_variants.name: prismatic_beta
habit_variants.trigger: 95<T<119°C — β-sulfur monoclinic prism / needle; RARE because it converts to α-S on cooling with internal cracking

habit_variants.name: sublimation_crust
habit_variants.trigger: high σ + T>60°C — fumarole gas-phase deposition; bright yellow crystalline crust (Vulcano Italy / El Desierto Bolivia)

native_sulfur.description: S — elemental sulfur, the bright canary-yellow native non-metal built from crown-shaped S₈ rings. Orthorhombic α-S (room T) or monoclinic β-S (T>95.5°C, unstable below). Mohs 1.5-2.5, fragile, thermally sensitive — a warm hand can crack a cool specimen. The synproportionation mineral: native S lives on the H₂S/SO₄²⁻ redox boundary where the reaction H₂S + SO₄²⁻ → 2S⁰ + H₂O drops elemental S out of solution. Forms in volcanic fumaroles (Vulcano, El Desierto), salt-dome caprocks via Desulfovibrio bacterial sulfate reduction (Tarnobrzeg), and Sicilian sulfur-vug evaporites with celestine companions (Agrigento — type locality, crystals to 20+ cm). Sources: research/research-native-sulfur.md (boss commit f2939da), Holland 1965 (Econ. Geol. 60).
native_sulfur.thermal_decomp_reaction: S (s) → S (l) — melts at 115.2°C; burns to SO₂ at ~250°C in air

twin_laws.name: contact_101
twin_laws._source: Anthony Handbook v.I. Native alpha-sulfur (orthorhombic Fddd, S) shows {101} and {011} contact twins documented in the classic Sicilian Cianciana + Agrigento bipyramidal crystals (the Sicilian "solfara" sulfur). Maybee + Bolivia (Tarapaca) also produce twinned specimens. p=0.02 reflects the minor-common visible-twin frequency in well-formed bipyramidal specimens.

native_sulfur.audit_status: Round 8b-2 (Apr 2026). Engine + supersaturation + nucleation + narrator complete in vugg.py + web/index.html; JSON entry mirrored to docs/. The synproportionation Eh-window engine: native S fires only when 0.1<O₂<0.7 (the H₂S/SO₄²⁻ boundary), pH<5 (H₂S dominant regime), and Fe+Cu+Pb+Zn<100 (base-metal sulfide capture gate). Habit variants: bipyramidal_alpha (T<95°C, Sicilian Agrigento), prismatic_beta (95-119°C, RARE — converts to α with cracking), sublimation_crust (high σ + T>60°C, fumarole habit). Engine includes oxidative-dissolution branch (O₂>0.9 returns S to fluid as SO₄²⁻) and per-step pH acidification (synproportionation releases minor H⁺). THERMAL_DECOMPOSITION at 115°C (melting point) — outside most scenario T ranges. Sources: Holland 1965 (Econ. Geol. 60), boss research file research/research-native-sulfur.md.

habit_variants.name: prismatic_hex
habit_variants.trigger: high T (>250°C) + low-mid σ — well-formed striated hexagonal prisms with {1010} faces and {1011} terminations; the Kalgoorlie habit

habit_variants.name: granular
habit_variants.trigger: moderate T + mid σ — Cripple Creek ore form, granular massive tin-white

habit_variants.name: reticulated
habit_variants.trigger: low T (<200°C) + mid σ — interconnected wire/filiform habit

native_tellurium.description: Te — elemental tellurium, the rarest of the residual-overflow native trio. Trigonal (P3₁21), Mohs 2-2.5, SG 6.2, perfect {1010} prismatic cleavage. Tin-white to steel-gray metallic, tarnishes to TeO₂ within days of exposure. Tellurium is rarer than platinum in Earth's crust; native Te only crystallizes when every telluride-forming metal (Au → calaverite/sylvanite, Ag → hessite, Pb → altaite, Bi → tetradymite, Hg → coloradoite) has had its fill — a relic of excess. Type localities: Cripple Creek (Colorado), Kalgoorlie (Western Australia, the richest Au-Te deposit on Earth), Emperor Mine (Vatukoula, Fiji). Sources: research/research-native-tellurium.md (boss commit f2939da), Spry & Thieben 1996 (Mineralium Deposita 31).
native_tellurium.thermal_decomp_reaction: Te (s) → Te (l) — melts at 449.5°C; boils at 988°C

twin_laws.name: contact_0112
twin_laws._source: Anthony Handbook v.I + Ramdohr 1980 §3 native elements. Native tellurium (trigonal P3121, Te) forms prismatic hexagonal crystals at the iconic Cresson Vug (Cripple Creek CO) and Săcărâmb (Romania) telluride zones. Rare {01-12} contact twins reported. Conservative p=0.005 (rare-twin floor) — most native tellurium is small-crystal or massive.

native_tellurium.audit_status: Round 8b-3 (Apr 2026). Engine + supersaturation + nucleation + narrator complete in vugg.py + web/index.html; JSON entry mirrored to docs/. The metal-telluride-overflow engine: hard gates at Au>1 (overflows to calaverite/sylvanite), Ag>5 (overflows to hessite), O₂>0.5 (overflows to tellurite/tellurate). Soft suppressors for Pb (altaite) and Bi (tetradymite). Hg not currently tracked in FluidChemistry; coloradoite (HgTe) gate deferred to a future Hg-plumbing round. Habit variants: prismatic_hex (high-T striated prisms, the Kalgoorlie museum habit), granular (Cripple Creek ore form), reticulated (low-T filiform). Engine includes oxidative-dissolution branch. THERMAL_DECOMPOSITION at 449°C melting point. Currently absent_at_seed_42: porphyry has Au=2 (above gate of >1) and Ag=8 (above gate of >5), so Te stays in solution destined for calaverite/sylvanite/hessite — those engines don't exist yet. The Au-Te coupling round (calaverite + sylvanite + hessite + altaite + tetradymite + coloradoite) is now unblocked — telluride engines are the natural Round 9 lift; once Au is locked in calaverite, residual Te will be free to fire as native. Sources: Spry & Thieben 1996 (Mineralium Deposita 31), boss research file research/research-native-tellurium.md.
native_tellurium._late_stage_note: Proposal C (2026-05): propensity:0.4. Cripple Creek Cresson Vug bonanza pockets (Spry & Thieben 1996): native_Te grains as inclusions WITHIN hessite — residual-overflow paragenesis at the tail end of the cascade.

habit_variants.name: massive_granular
habit_variants.trigger: low σ — granular massive ore form, the dominant habit

habit_variants.name: columnar
habit_variants.trigger: moderate σ — vertical hexagonal {0001} aggregates

habit_variants.name: reniform
habit_variants.trigger: high σ — botryoidal copper-red crusts with concentric layers, the Cobalt-Ontario signature

nickeline.description: NiAs — hexagonal nickel arsenide, the namesake of the NiAs structure-type. Pale copper-red metallic on fresh fracture, the diagnostic color that fooled early miners into mistaking it for copper ore (the 'kupfernickel' = copper-devil etymology). Mohs 5-5.5, SG 7.8. Forms in high-T reducing veins where Ni and As are present together; cooler / As-poor regimes push the chemistry to millerite (NiS) or annabergite (the supergene Ni arsenate). Type-locality habit at Cobalt, Ontario; also documented at Schneeberg (Saxony), Příbram (Czech), and Tunaberg (Sweden). Sources: research/research-nickeline.md (boss commit f2939da), Petruk 1971.

twin_laws.name: contact_1011
twin_laws._source: Ramdohr 1980 §4.5 nickel-arsenide section + Anthony Handbook v.I. Nickeline (NiAs, hexagonal P6₃/mmc) shows {10-11} contact twins at Cobalt (Ontario) and Schneeberg (Saxony). Conservative p=0.02 — most nickeline occurs as massive replacement of cobalt-nickel veins; visible euhedral twins are uncommon.

nickeline.audit_status: Round 8c-2 (Apr 2026). Engine + supersaturation + nucleation + narrator complete in vugg.py + web/index.html; JSON entry mirrored to docs/. Ni+As required + reducing (O₂<0.6) + T 200-500°C with peak 300-450. Substrate preference: arsenopyrite (the paragenetic predecessor). Engine includes oxidative-dissolution branch that releases Ni²⁺ + AsO₄³⁻ to fluid, feeding the supergene annabergite cascade. Activates with Round 8c-1 Bisbee Ni=70 + As=8 chemistry; expected to fire in seed-42 Bisbee primary stages and supergene_oxidation supergene cascade. Mutual-priority dispatcher with millerite: when As>30 + T>200, nickeline takes priority (NiAs more stable than NiS at high-T As-saturation). Sources: Petruk 1971 (Co-Ni-Ag paragenesis), boss research file research/research-nickeline.md.

habit_variants.name: massive
habit_variants.trigger: low σ — granular ore form, the bulk habit

habit_variants.name: acicular
habit_variants.trigger: mid σ — slender brass-yellow prismatic needles

habit_variants.name: capillary
habit_variants.trigger: high σ + As-poor fluid — hair-fine acicular needles in radiating sprays, the Halls Gap geode habit; the most distinctive millerite morphology

millerite.description: NiS — trigonal nickel sulfide, brass-yellow to bronze-yellow metallic. Mohs 3-3.5, the rare nickel mineral that grows as hair-thin acicular needles in geode cavities. Forms in lower-T regimes than nickeline (NiAs); when As is depleted and Ni + S remain, NiS takes the field. The capillary habit is so distinctive that 'millerite' is shorthand among collectors for any radiating-needle sulfide. Showcase localities: Halls Gap (Kentucky), Sterling Hill (NJ), Glamorgan (Wales). Sources: research/research-millerite.md (boss commit f2939da), Bayliss 1969 (Geochim. Cosmochim. Acta 33).

twin_laws.name: contact_3034
twin_laws._source: Anthony Handbook v.I + Mindat millerite habit notes. Millerite (NiS, trigonal R3m) is famous for hair-fine acicular tufts (Sterling Mine, Iowa, the classic geode locality). Twinning on {30-34} is documented but vanishingly rare — most millerite is capillary and untwinned. Conservative p=0.005 (rare-twin floor with data-sparse caveat).

millerite.audit_status: Round 8c-3 (Apr 2026). Engine + supersaturation + nucleation + narrator complete in vugg.py + web/index.html; JSON entry mirrored to docs/. Ni+S required + reducing (O₂<0.6) + T 100-400°C with peak 200-350. Mutual-exclusion gate with nickeline: when As>30 AND T>200, returns 0 (nickeline takes priority — NiAs more stable than NiS at high-T As-saturation). The capillary habit (hair-fine brass-yellow needles in radiating sprays) is the diagnostic morphology — Halls Gap KY, Sterling Hill NJ, Glamorgan Wales produce showcase specimens. Substrate preference: pyrite (the geological companion). Activates with Round 8c-1 Bisbee Ni=70 chemistry; expected to fire in supergene_oxidation when As is depleted from earlier scorodite/erythrite/annabergite. Sources: Bayliss 1969 (NiS-NiAs equilibria), boss research file research/research-millerite.md.

habit_variants.name: massive_granular
habit_variants.trigger: low σ — granular ore form, tin-white-with-pink-blush

habit_variants.name: pyritohedral
habit_variants.trigger: mid σ — {210} pyritohedral crystals with diagnostic striations parallel to cube edges; the Cobalt Ontario primary habit

habit_variants.name: reniform
habit_variants.trigger: high σ — botryoidal crusts when supersaturation outruns diffusion-limited growth

cobaltite.description: CoAsS — orthorhombic cobalt sulfarsenide (pseudo-cubic, pyritohedral habit). Tin-white with diagnostic pinkish blush from surface Co oxidation, Mohs 5.5. The cobalt analog of arsenopyrite. The three-element gate (Co + As + S all required simultaneously) makes this rare; the Cobalt-Ontario type locality is one of the few places it forms in well-developed crystals. Indian jewellers used powdered cobaltite ('sehta') to make blue enamel on gold ornaments — the source of the first Co ever isolated from a non-arsenide mineral. Sources: research/research-cobaltite.md (boss commit f2939da), Bayliss 1968 (Mineral. Mag. 36, on cobaltite-arsenopyrite substitution).

twin_laws.name: iron_cross
twin_laws._source: Anthony Handbook v.I + Ramdohr 1980 §4.5 cobalt-arsenide section. Cobaltite is pseudo-cubic (orthorhombic Pca2₁ but cubic in habit) and shares pyritohedral / iron-cross-like twinning behavior with the pyrite group. Documented at Tunaberg (Sweden), Cobalt (Ontario). Conservative p=0.02 — twins visible in well-formed specimens but not common.

cobaltite.audit_status: Round 8c-4 (Apr 2026). Engine + supersaturation + nucleation + narrator complete in vugg.py + web/index.html; JSON entry mirrored to docs/. THREE-ELEMENT GATE engine: Co + As + S all required simultaneously at minimum thresholds (Co≥50, As≥100, S≥50). First three-element gate in the sim. Reducing (O₂<0.5) + T 300-600°C with peak 400-500. Substrate preference: arsenopyrite (the paragenetic predecessor). Habit variants: massive_granular (low σ, ore form), pyritohedral (mid σ, {210} striated faces — Cobalt Ontario diagnostic), reniform (high σ, botryoidal). Glaucodot series captured in narrator: Fe>100 in fluid yields '(Co,Fe)AsS' note — the iron-rich end of the cobaltite-glaucodot solid solution (Skutterud type). Engine includes oxidative-dissolution branch: when O₂>0.7, releases Co²⁺ + AsO₄³⁻ + SO₄²⁻ to fluid, feeding the existing erythrite engine downstream — this is THE primary source for the magenta-pink 'cobalt bloom' supergene cascade. Activates with Round 8c-1 Bisbee Co=80 + As=8 + S=150 chemistry; expected to fire in seed-42 Bisbee primary stage. Sources: Bayliss 1968 (cobaltite-arsenopyrite substitution), boss research file research/research-cobaltite.md.

descloizite.description: Pb(Zn,Cu)VO₄(OH) — orthorhombic Pb-Zn vanadate, the Zn end of the descloizite-mottramite series. Cherry-red to brown-red, Mohs 3-3.5; the Tsumeb display standard. Forms in supergene oxidation zones where Pb-Zn sulfide ore (galena + sphalerite) has weathered AND V is delivered by groundwater (red-bed roll-front signature). Cu/Zn ratio dispatch: when Cu > Zn, mottramite (the olive-green Cu sibling) takes priority instead. Sources: research/research-descloizite.md (boss commit f2939da), Strunz 1959 (Tsumeb monograph).
descloizite._ingredients_note: Round 9c (Apr 2026): Cu=0.5 is required by the broth-ratio engine retrofit so the Cu:Zn ratio is meaningful. Real descloizite always has at least trace Cu (cuprian descloizite — common at Tsumeb and Berg Aukas).

twin_laws.name: contact_110
twin_laws._source: Anthony Handbook v.IV (vanadates) + Frondel 1958. Descloizite (orthorhombic Pnma, PbZn(VO₄)(OH)) shows {110} contact twins documented at Berg Aukas (Namibia) and Sierra de Cordoba (Argentina). Prismatic crystals with sector-pair twins. Conservative p=0.02 (rare-to-minor in field specimens).

descloizite.audit_status: Round 8d-1a (Apr 2026). Engine + supersaturation + nucleation + narrator complete in vugg.py + web/index.html. Cu/Zn ratio dispatch with mottramite (when Cu>Zn → mottramite, when Zn≥Cu → descloizite). Pb+Zn+V+oxidizing, T 20-80°C peak 30-50. Substrate preference: vanadinite (the same V source). Fires at seed-42 in supergene_oxidation when Tsumeb's Pb=60 + Zn=90 + V=1.5+events meet the gates. Source: Strunz 1959, boss research file.

mottramite.description: Pb(Cu,Zn)VO₄(OH) — orthorhombic Pb-Cu vanadate, the Cu end of the descloizite-mottramite series. Olive-green to yellowish-green from the Cu chromophore (distinguishing it at a glance from cherry-red descloizite), Mohs 3-3.5. Type locality: Mottram St. Andrew, Cheshire (England), 1876. Tsumeb produces the museum-grade specimens. Cu/Zn ratio dispatch: when Zn ≥ Cu, descloizite takes priority. Sources: research/research-mottramite.md (boss commit f2939da).
mottramite._ingredients_note: Round 9c (Apr 2026): Zn=0.5 is required by the broth-ratio engine retrofit so the Cu:Zn ratio is meaningful. Real mottramite always has at least trace Zn (zincian mottramite).

twin_laws.name: contact_110
twin_laws._source: Anthony Handbook v.IV. Isostructural with descloizite (orthorhombic Pnma, Cu endmember). Same twin behavior; documented at Mottram St Andrew (England, type locality) and Tsumeb. Conservative p=0.02 matching descloizite.

mottramite.audit_status: Round 8d-1b (Apr 2026). Engine + supersaturation + nucleation + narrator complete in vugg.py + web/index.html. Cu/Zn ratio dispatch with descloizite (when Cu>Zn → mottramite). Pb+Cu+V+oxidizing, T 20-80°C peak 30-50. Substrate preference: vanadinite. Source: boss research file research-mottramite.md.

habit_variants.name: tabular_monoclinic
habit_variants.trigger: the only habit — RARE monoclinic PbWO₄ with perfect {100} cleavage; honey-yellow tabular plates

raspite.description: PbWO₄ — monoclinic lead tungstate, RARE. Same composition as stolzite but a different crystal system; stolzite (tetragonal) is favored ~90% of the time when both can form (kinetic-preference dispatcher in check_nucleation reflects natural rarity). Honey-yellow to brownish-yellow tabular crystals, Mohs 2.5-3, perfect {100} cleavage. Type locality: Broken Hill, NSW, Australia. Sources: research/research-raspite.md (boss commit f2939da).

twin_laws.name: contact_100
twin_laws._source: Anthony Handbook v.V. Raspite (monoclinic P21/n, PbWO4) is the monoclinic dimorph of stolzite — rare at most localities, dominant at Broken Hill (NSW, Australia type). Tabular habit shows {100} contact twins per Anthony. Conservative p=0.02 reflecting the visible twins among well-formed Broken Hill specimens.

raspite.audit_status: Round 8d-2a (Apr 2026). Engine + supersaturation + nucleation + narrator complete in vugg.py + web/index.html. Same Pb+W+oxidizing chemistry as stolzite but narrower T window (10-50°C, peak 20-40); sigma threshold 1.4 (vs stolzite's 1.0) reflects rarity. Kinetic-preference dispatcher in check_nucleation: when both raspite + stolzite gates clear, stolzite wins ~90% of rolls (random.random() < 0.9 zeros raspite's sigma). Tsumeb scenario W=20 plumbing activates the engine. Source: boss research file.

habit_variants.name: dipyramidal
habit_variants.trigger: high σ — {101} tetragonal dipyramid, the Broken Hill / Tsumeb display habit

habit_variants.name: tabular_tetragonal
habit_variants.trigger: low σ — {001} tabular plates, late-stage habit

stolzite.description: PbWO₄ — tetragonal lead tungstate, the lead analog of scheelite (CaWO₄). Honey-yellow to orange-yellow Mohs 2.5-3, the more common of the two PbWO₄ polymorphs (~90% of natural occurrences vs raspite's monoclinic ~10%). Type locality: Cínovec (Czech Republic). Broken Hill (Australia) and Tsumeb (Namibia) produce the museum specimens — sharp honey-yellow dipyramids. Sources: research/research-stolzite.md (boss commit f2939da).

twin_laws.name: contact_110
twin_laws._source: Anthony Handbook v.V. Stolzite (tetragonal I41/a, PbWO4) is the tetragonal scheelite-group dimorph of raspite. Same Broken Hill type-locality; also documented at Tsumeb + Mapimí. {110} contact twins per Anthony — scheelite-group dipyramidal habit favors visible twin formation. p=0.02 matching raspite.

stolzite.audit_status: Round 8d-2b (Apr 2026). Engine + supersaturation + nucleation + narrator complete in vugg.py + web/index.html. Same Pb+W+oxidizing chemistry as raspite, broader T window (10-100°C, peak 20-80) reflecting tetragonal stability range. Tsumeb scenario W=20 plumbing activates both raspite + stolzite gates; the kinetic dispatcher gives stolzite the win ~90% of the time. Source: boss research file.
