Triclinic {100} polysynthetic twinning is the standard pyroxenoid-group law (shared with wollastonite, bustamite, rhodonite). Reported in New Jersey + Larimar specimens at 5-10% frequency among euhedral fibers.

acid_dissolution.reaction: NaCa2Si3O8(OH) + 7H⁺ → Na⁺ + 2Ca²⁺ + 3H4SiO4 — releases Na + Ca + Si to fluid below pH 6. Pectolite is more acid-sensitive than wollastonite/prehnite (Na presence).

white_spray.trigger: no significant chromophore — the default Jeffrey + basalt-amygdale aesthetic

blue_larimar.trigger: Cu > 0.5 ppm — Dominican Republic gem variety (Cu²⁺-O charge transfer)

pectolite.audit_status: new v113 (2026-05-20). Triclinic Na-Ca inosilicate, fourth mineral of the Jeffrey Mine rodingite arc; iconic radiating-spray habit. Substrate priority: grossular (the Jeffrey signature) > diopside > vesuvianite > calcite > wall. Cu trace dispatch gives larimar tint (separate cluster from the Jeffrey aesthetic but same engine). CALIBRATION expectation: pectolite may fire in late-stage skarn/amygdale scenarios where Na is high; first firing expected at jeffrey_mine v115. Per Anthony Handbook v.IIB; Filipos & Frantz 1979; Bernardini 1981.
pectolite._late_stage_note: Late-stage in the rodingite paragenesis — pectolite forms AFTER grossular + diopside + vesuvianite, contemporaneous with wollastonite, before terminal datolite. In basalt amygdales, pectolite is one of the LAST minerals to fire in the alteration sequence. Propensity 0.7 = late.

habit_variants.name: acicular_white
habit_variants.trigger: default — acicular needles in parallel bundles. The standard skarn + rodingite morphology.

habit_variants.name: fibrous_sprays
habit_variants.trigger: high supersaturation (excess > 1.0) — radiating acicular fibrous sprays, silky luster. The high-grade skarn cabinet form.

habit_variants.name: massive_granular
habit_variants.trigger: low supersaturation (excess < 0.4) — massive granular aggregate, the workhorse skarn texture (industrially mined as ceramic filler).

wollastonite.description: CaSiO₃ — triclinic single-chain Ca-silicate (inosilicate). The simplest Ca-Si stoichiometry of the calc-silicate suite. H 4.5-5, density 2.86-3.09, vitreous to silky luster, perfect {100} + {001} cleavage. Forms in three settings: (1) SKARN contact-metamorphism of impure limestone (the dominant occurrence — Crestmore CA, Willsboro NY, Helan Mountains China, Pakistan, Mexico Sierra de Cruces; INDUSTRIALLY mined globally as a ceramic filler + asbestos replacement at ~700,000 tonnes/year world production), (2) Rodingite metasomatism (Jeffrey Mine — acicular sprays + radiating aggregates per Bernardini 1981 MR 12(5):277; usually as the late-stage Ca-Si endmember after grossular + diopside), (3) Carbonatite alteration (rare). Pseudohexagonal twinning common. Discriminator from analogous Ca-silicates: pectolite has Na + OH (wollastonite has neither); diopside has Mg + chain doubling; grossular is the orthosilicate garnet (different structural class). Refs: Anthony Handbook v.IIB; Deer Howie Zussman 2A; Trommsdorff V & Connolly JAD (1996) The ultramafic contact aureole about the Bregaglia (Bergell) tonalite. Schweiz. Mineral. Petrogr. Mitt. 76:135 (wollastonite-isograd phase relations).
wollastonite._ingredients_note: Ca ≥ 80 + SiO2 ≥ 200 — simplest gates of the calc-silicate suite. T 180-600°C wide window; sweet spot 220-400°C. pH 7.5-12 broad alkaline tolerance. Mg > 100 marginally suppresses (Mg-rich systems favor diopside); Al > 50 marginally suppresses (Al-rich systems favor grossular). Activity correction applied.

trace_ingredients.Fe: rare trace gives pale-pink tint (parawollastonite or pseudowollastonite forms; not modeled separately here)
trace_ingredients.Mn: rare trace gives bustamite-similar pink (separate species, not wollastonite proper)

wollastonite.thermal_decomp_reaction: Above ~1125°C: wollastonite (α-CaSiO3) inverts to pseudowollastonite (β-CaSiO3) — the high-T polymorph; geologically irrelevant for vug-formation regime.

fluorescence.status: Most wollastonite is non-fluorescent. Mn-bearing material may show weak red-orange SW UV (Mn²⁺ activator) but rare and not diagnostic. Ignored in the engine.

twin_laws.trigger: common pseudohexagonal twinning in skarn + rodingite wollastonite

acid_dissolution.reaction: CaSiO3 + 2H⁺ + H2O → Ca²⁺ + H4SiO4 — releases Ca + Si to fluid below pH 5. Moderate kinetics; wollastonite is one of the more reactive calc-silicates.

white.trigger: no significant chromophore — pure Ca-Si endmember

wollastonite.audit_status: new v113 (2026-05-20). Triclinic Ca-Si inosilicate, fifth mineral of the Jeffrey Mine rodingite arc; the simplest Ca-Si endmember. Substrate priority: grossular > diopside > calcite > wall. CALIBRATION expectation: wollastonite may fire in skarn scenarios (marble_contact_metamorphism is a candidate) since the gates are simpler than the rodingite-specific Mg/Al-rich requirements. First firing expected in v115 jeffrey_mine; possibly also marble_contact_metamorphism cascade. Per Anthony Handbook v.IIB; Trommsdorff & Connolly 1996.
wollastonite._late_stage_note: Mid-to-late stage in the prograde sequence. Forms after grossular + diopside as Ca-Si chemistry concentrates; contemporaneous with pectolite + prehnite in rodingite. In skarn, wollastonite is the workhorse late-stage Ca-Si phase.

habit_variants.name: botryoidal_pale_green
habit_variants.trigger: default — botryoidal grape-cluster pale-green aggregate, subradial fibrous internal structure. The Lake Superior amygdale + Alpine + Italian classic.

habit_variants.name: tabular_crystallized
habit_variants.trigger: high supersaturation (excess > 1.4) — tabular flat {001} tablets, orthorhombic crystallized cabinet form. Often partial radial bundles.

habit_variants.name: reniform
habit_variants.trigger: low supersaturation (excess < 0.3) — reniform pale-green crust, low-relief surface coating.

prehnite.description: Ca₂Al₂Si₃O₁₀(OH)₂ — orthorhombic Ca-Al phyllosilicate (sheet silicate). H 6-6.5, density 2.80-2.95, vitreous luster, pale-green characteristic color from Fe³⁺ trace. The CLASSIC pale-green botryoidal habit is one of the most-recognized cabinet aesthetics — Lake Superior basalt amygdales (Keweenaw + Isle Royale; Bornhorst 2017 GSA Memoir 213), Iceland amygdales, Alpine fissures (Switzerland Tauern + French Alps), Italian Val di Fassa, rodingite at Jeffrey Mine Quebec + Outokumpu Finland. Important substrate for: datolite + epidote + zeolite-group minerals (heulandite + stilbite + analcime). Discriminator from analogous Ca-Al silicates: zoisite/epidote have +Fe in the Y site; vesuvianite has +Mg; lawsonite is the higher-P endmember (subduction); grossular is the orthosilicate garnet. The pale-green color is dominantly from Fe³⁺ trace (5-50 ppm) — pure Ca-Al prehnite is white-colorless but is rare in collected specimens. Refs: Anthony Handbook v.IIA Phyllosilicates; Deer Howie Zussman v.1B; Liou JG (1971) Synthesis and stability relations of prehnite, Ca2Al2Si3O10(OH)2. American Mineralogist 56:507-531 (definitive stability + zeolite-facies parageneses); Bornhorst TJ 2017 GSA Memoir 213.
prehnite._ingredients_note: Ca ≥ 60 + Al ≥ 8 + SiO2 ≥ 100 — moderate-threshold Ca-Al-Si silicate. T 100-350°C; sweet spot 150-280°C (zeolite-facies + low-grade greenschist). pH 7.5-11.5 alkaline. Activity correction applied.

trace_ingredients.Fe: 5-50 ppm trace gives pale-green (default field aesthetic — Fe³⁺ d-d transitions; the diagnostic Lake Superior + Alpine color)
trace_ingredients.Cu: 2+ ppm gives apple-green with blue tint (rare; sometimes called 'copper-bearing prehnite')

prehnite.thermal_decomp_reaction: Above ~400°C: prehnite decomposes via dehydration to anorthite + wollastonite + H2O. Marks the upper-T boundary of the zeolite-facies regime per Liou 1971.

fluorescence.status: Generally non-fluorescent. Fe-rich varieties may show weak fluorescence; not diagnostic.

twin_laws.name: polysynthetic_001
twin_laws._source: Dana 8th ed. prehnite section; Akizuki (1987) American Mineralogist 72:645 on prehnite structural state + growth twins. Orthorhombic {001} polysynthetic twin reported in zeolite-cavity prehnite from Bombay basalts + New Jersey traprock at moderate frequency. Conservative p=0.02.

acid_dissolution.reaction: Ca2Al2Si3O10(OH)2 + 10H⁺ → 2Ca²⁺ + 2Al³⁺ + 3H4SiO4 — releases Ca + Al + Si to fluid below pH 6. Moderate kinetics; prehnite is reactive in acidic groundwater.

pale_green.trigger: Fe trace > 5 ppm — Lake Superior + Alpine + Italian default aesthetic; Fe³⁺ d-d transitions

colorless_white.trigger: Fe < 5 ppm — pure Ca-Al endmember; rare in collected material

prehnite.audit_status: new v113 (2026-05-20). Orthorhombic Ca-Al phyllosilicate, sixth mineral of the Jeffrey Mine rodingite arc + Lake Superior basalt-amygdale classic. Substrate priority: native_copper (Lake Superior signature) > grossular > diopside > calcite > wall. Will activate the forward-prepared substrate slot for v110 datolite (prehnite > wollastonite > vesuvianite > calcite > ...). CALIBRATION expectation: prehnite may fire in zeolite-facies + low-grade-metamorphic existing scenarios (zoned_dripstone_cave, marble_contact_metamorphism candidates); first deliberate firing expected at v115 jeffrey_mine. Per Anthony Handbook v.IIA; Liou 1971 Am. Min. 56:507; Bornhorst 2017 GSA Memoir 213.
prehnite._late_stage_note: Mid-to-late stage in the rodingite paragenesis. Prehnite forms after grossular + diopside + vesuvianite but before terminal datolite + zeolites. In Lake Superior amygdales, prehnite is one of the EARLY-to-mid amygdale fillings (with calcite + analcime + native_copper); the rodingite paragenesis pushes it later.

habit_variants.name: fibrous
habit_variants.trigger: moderate-high excess (> 0.6) — parallel-bundle silky fibers, the asbestos aesthetic + cabinet diagnostic. Commercial chrysotile-asbestos texture.

habit_variants.name: massive_fibrous
habit_variants.trigger: low-moderate excess (0.2-0.6) — compact massive serpentinite texture, subradiating fiber bundles internally.

habit_variants.name: platy
habit_variants.trigger: very low excess (< 0.2) — platy texture (lizardite-similar), low-relief serpentine coating.

chrysotile.description: Mg₃Si₂O₅(OH)₄ — monoclinic serpentine-group phyllosilicate (clinochrysotile, the most common polytype; ortho- and parachrysotile are minor polytypes). H 2.5-3.5, density 2.55, silky-greasy luster, conchoidal fracture for massive form. THE asbestos of commerce — fibrous habit diagnostic with parallel-bundle silky fibers. Forms by SERPENTINIZATION: olivine + pyroxene + H₂O → serpentine minerals at T 200-500°C and pH 10-12, in ultramafic protolith (peridotite/dunite/harzburgite). Jeffrey Mine (Val-des-Sources Quebec — formerly named Asbestos QC; 1881-2011 produced ~40% of world chrysotile-asbestos; town renamed 2020). Other major producers: Thetford Mines QC, Cassiar BC, Coalinga CA, Sverdlovsk Russia, Zhetygara Kazakhstan. The asbestos health concerns (mesothelioma + asbestosis from prolonged inhalation, the 1949 Quebec Asbestos Strike etc.) are real but secondary to the geological story — at Jeffrey, the rodingite-contact cabinet assemblage is what mineral collectors value. Chrysotile is the host-matrix mineral; the rodingite minerals (vesuvianite cyprine + grossular + diopside + pectolite + datolite) crystallize within and along the chrysotile-serpentine veining. Discriminator from analogous serpentines: lizardite is platy (no fibers), antigorite is corrugated-platy (no straight fibers); chrysotile is the only true fibrous polytype. Refs: Anthony JW et al. Handbook of Mineralogy v.IIA Phyllosilicates; Deer WA, Howie RA, Zussman J Rock-Forming Minerals v.1B; Wicks FJ & Plant AG (1979) Electron-microprobe + TEM study of serpentine minerals. Canadian Mineralogist 17:785-830; O'Hanley DS (1996) Serpentinites: Records of Tectonic and Petrological History. Oxford. THE rodingite + serpentine framework. Bernardini GP (1981) MR 12(5):277.
chrysotile._ingredients_note: Mg ≥ 100 (serpentinization Mg released from olivine + pyroxene of ultramafic protolith) + SiO2 ≥ 50 (silica framework). T 50-500°C; sweet spot 200-400°C (the serpentinization window per Wicks & Plant 1979). pH 8.5-13 strict alkaline (hyperalkaline serpentinization-driven fluid). Ca > 100 marginally suppresses chrysotile in favor of diopside/wollastonite (Ca-Mg-Si systems route Mg to clinopyroxene).

trace_ingredients.Ni: common substitution at Mg sites in serpentine; can produce pale yellow-green tint at higher Ni

chrysotile.thermal_decomp_reaction: Above ~500°C: chrysotile dehydrates and recrystallizes to forsterite + talc + H2O, then ultimately to olivine + enstatite + H2O at higher T per O'Hanley 1996. Marks the upper-T boundary of serpentine stability.

fluorescence.status: Non-fluorescent.

chrysotile._twin_laws_note: Intentionally empty — chrysotile is the fibrous polytype of the serpentine group (the asbestos variety). Forms ONLY as parallel-aligned fibrous bundles or platy aggregates; no individual euhedral crystals to twin. Per Hawthorne et al. 2012 + Veblen 1980 (Reviews in Mineralogy v.19 amphibole/serpentine).

acid_dissolution.reaction: Mg3Si2O5(OH)4 + 6H⁺ → 3Mg²⁺ + 2H4SiO4 + H2O — releases Mg + Si to fluid below pH 7. Below serpentine stability field; slow kinetics in field environments.

white_pale_green.trigger: default — white to pale-yellow-green serpentine (the field aesthetic for both vein chrysotile and commercial asbestos)

chrysotile.audit_status: new v114 (2026-05-20). Monoclinic serpentine-group phyllosilicate, seventh mineral of the Jeffrey Mine rodingite arc; the asbestos of commerce + Jeffrey host-matrix mineral. Substrate priority: magnetite > wall. Max nucleation count 5 (host-matrix mineral; many crystals expected). CALIBRATION expectation: chrysotile may fire in ultramafic_supergene scenario which already carries Mg + alkaline pH; first deliberate firing at v115 jeffrey_mine. Per Anthony Handbook v.IIA; Wicks & Plant 1979; O'Hanley 1996; Bernardini 1981.
chrysotile._late_stage_note: EARLY-stage host matrix — chrysotile forms FIRST in the rodingite paragenesis (the serpentinization event), then the rodingite-contact minerals crystallize within and along chrysotile veining. Propensity 0.2 = very early.

habit_variants.name: tabular_hexagonal
habit_variants.trigger: default — flat hexagonal {0001} tabular plates.

habit_variants.name: pearly_lamellae
habit_variants.trigger: high excess (> 1.2) — pearly-vitreous flat {0001} cleavage flakes, partial bunched lamellar morphology. Best-of-cabinet from Asbestos QC + Wood's Mine PA.

habit_variants.name: foliated_mass
habit_variants.trigger: low excess (< 0.3) — compact foliated massive, no terminations, the field aesthetic.

brucite.description: Mg(OH)₂ — trigonal Mg hydroxide. H 2.5, density 2.39, pearly to vitreous luster, perfect {0001} cleavage producing flexible lamellae. The serpentinization byproduct mineral — when olivine + pyroxene + H₂O react to form serpentine, excess Mg precipitates as brucite. Diagnostic hyperalkaline pH (10-13), above the magnesite-carbonate stability boundary. Common at Jeffrey Mine Quebec (associated with chrysotile veining), Italian Alps Val Malenco, Marmara Turkey, Mt. Lewis NV, Wood's Mine PA (the type-locality cabinet specimens). Distinct from carbonate-substituted hydromagnesite (Mg-OH-CO3, separate mineral; brucite has no CO3). Refs: Anthony JW et al. Handbook of Mineralogy v.III; Deer Howie Zussman v.5 Non-Silicates; O'Hanley DS (1996) Serpentinites: Records of Tectonic and Petrological History. Oxford; Schramke JA, Kerrick DM, Lasaga AC (1982) Calculation of the dehydration reaction muscovite + quartz = K-feldspar + sillimanite + water (and brucite stability experiments). Geochim. Cosmochim. Acta 46:1581.
brucite._ingredients_note: Mg ≥ 100 — simplest stoichiometry. T 30-450°C; sweet spot 100-300°C. pH 9.5-13.5 STRICT hyperalkaline. CO3 > 50 ppm gates out (brucite + CO2 → magnesite + H2O carbonatization).

trace_ingredients.Fe: trace gives pale-green tint (Fe³⁺ d-d) — sometimes called 'iron brucite'

brucite.thermal_decomp_reaction: Above ~350°C: brucite dehydrates to periclase MgO + H2O. Common laboratory test for brucite.

fluorescence.status: Non-fluorescent.

twin_laws.name: contact_1011
twin_laws._source: Anthony Handbook v.V brucite section. Brucite (trigonal P-3m1, Mg(OH)2) forms tabular hexagonal platelets at Hoboken (NJ, type) + Wood's Chrome Mine (PA) + Asbestos (Quebec). Layered structure with perfect {0001} cleavage; rare {10-11} contact twins reported. Most brucite is foliated/platy aggregates. p=0.005 (rare-twin floor — similar treatment to covellite + molybdenite layered minerals at v137).

acid_dissolution.reaction: Mg(OH)2 + 2H⁺ → Mg²⁺ + 2H2O — releases Mg + alkalinity to fluid below pH 8.5. Fast kinetics; brucite is one of the most acid-reactive Mg minerals.

white_pale_blue_green.trigger: default — white to pale-blue-green; Mn or Fe trace shifts toward green

brucite.audit_status: new v114 (2026-05-20). Trigonal Mg hydroxide, eighth mineral of the Jeffrey Mine rodingite arc + serpentinization byproduct. Oxide-class (hydroxides grouped with oxides per simulator convention). Substrate priority: chrysotile (rodingite host matrix) > magnetite > wall. CALIBRATION expectation: brucite may fire in ultramafic_supergene if pH is high enough; first deliberate firing at v115 jeffrey_mine. Per Anthony Handbook v.III; O'Hanley 1996; Schramke et al. 1982.
brucite._late_stage_note: Early-stage serpentinization byproduct, contemporaneous with chrysotile. Forms before the rodingite calc-silicates. Propensity 0.3 = early.

habit_variants.name: grains_microscopic
habit_variants.trigger: default — micron-scale grains in chrysotile/serpentine matrix, rarely visible without microscopy.

habit_variants.name: nuggets_rare
habit_variants.trigger: high excess (> 1.4) — mm-scale visible alloy grains, the Cassiar BC best-material aesthetic.

habit_variants.name: placer_grains
habit_variants.trigger: low T (< 120°C) + moderate excess — secondary placer concentration habit (Awaroa NZ + New Caledonia surface deposits).

awaruite.description: (Ni,Fe) — Ni-Fe alloy, ranging from Ni₂Fe to Ni₃Fe stoichiometry. Cubic Fm-3m, H 5, malleable, magnetic, metallic-silvery luster. THE serpentinization-driven natural metal. Forms when olivine + pyroxene of ultramafic protolith serpentinize under strongly reducing (O₂ < 0.3) and hyperalkaline (pH 9-13) conditions, releasing Ni and Fe in metallic-alloy form. Type locality AWAROA NZ 1885 (the namesake); MICROSCOPIC grains in serpentine + chrysotile matrix at most occurrences — rarely visible without microscopy. Larger nuggets at exceptional localities: Cassiar BC 1-2 mm grains; Bou Azzer Morocco; Jeffrey + Italian Alps; New Caledonia placers (secondary surface concentration). Coexists with magnetite + chrysotile + chromite in serpentinite matrix. The Ni in FluidChemistry was already added pre-v89 (speculative for millerite + annabergite + pentlandite + chrysoprase coloration); awaruite is the latest consumer. Discriminator: STRICT reducing + alkaline + S-free gate; sulfides (millerite NiS, pentlandite (Fe,Ni)9S8, heazlewoodite Ni3S2) win the Ni budget when S > 5 ppm. Refs: Anthony Handbook v.I; Bird DK & Bassett WA (1980) Fluid inclusion study of an active geothermal system Hekla Iceland (with Fe-Ni alloy stability discussion). Geochim. Cosmochim. Acta 44:1659; Krenn K & Hauzenberger CA (2007) Tonga ophiolite Fe-Ni alloy thermometry; Frost BR (1985) On the stability of sulfides, oxides and native metals in serpentinite. Contributions to Mineralogy and Petrology 91:139-153 (oxygen + sulfur fugacity controls).
awaruite._ingredients_note: Ni ≥ 50 + Fe ≥ 20 + STRICT reducing (nativeRedoxAnoxic with 0.3 threshold) + alkaline pH 9-13 + S < 5 ppm (sulfides take Ni when S is higher). T 50-500°C; sweet spot 200-400°C (serpentinization window).

trace_ingredients.Co: common minor substitution at Ni site
trace_ingredients.Cu: very rare trace at high Cu (would shift toward Cu-Ni-Fe alloy compositions)

awaruite.thermal_decomp_reaction: Above ~1450°C: awaruite melts. Below melting, stable in serpentinite + ultramafic systems indefinitely.

fluorescence.status: Non-fluorescent (metal).

awaruite._twin_laws_note: Intentionally empty — awaruite (cubic Pm-3m, Ni2Fe to Ni3Fe alloy) habit code 'grains_microscopic' — the species occurs only as microscopic grains in serpentinite, never as visible individual crystals at vugg-scale. No twin observation possible at this scale. v141 final batch.

acid_dissolution.reaction: Acid attack on awaruite proceeds via O2 + H+ → Ni²⁺ + Fe²⁺/Fe³⁺ → migration into oxide/silicate phases. Slow at the alkaline pH where awaruite is geologically stable.

silvery_metallic.trigger: metallic-silvery Ni-Fe alloy; magnetic; the universal awaruite aesthetic

awaruite.audit_status: new v114 (2026-05-20). Cubic Ni-Fe alloy, ninth mineral of the Jeffrey Mine rodingite arc. Native class. Substrate priority: chrysotile (rodingite host) > magnetite > wall. STRICT gates: reducing + alkaline + S-free. Per the v110 lesson, Ni was already in FluidChemistry (pre-v89 speculative) so no add-broth needed — awaruite ships as a normal vugg-add-mineral. CALIBRATION expectation: awaruite is unlikely to fire in any existing scenario due to its strict combination of reducing + alkaline + low-S; first firing expected at v115 jeffrey_mine. Per Anthony Handbook v.I; Bird & Bassett 1980; Frost 1985; Krenn & Hauzenberger 2007.
awaruite._late_stage_note: Early-to-mid stage in the serpentinization sequence — awaruite forms as alloy droplets during chrysotile precipitation. Contemporaneous with magnetite + chromite as ultramafic-protolith re-equilibration products.

habit_variants.name: fibrous_asbestiform
habit_variants.trigger: high σ (excess > 1.4) — parallel-bundle silky white fibers covering the cavity wall as a mat. The WHO-recognized health-hazard asbestos variety; the Jeffrey Mine chrysotile contaminant. Renders as wide wall area (per boss v116 guidance).

habit_variants.name: bladed_columnar
habit_variants.trigger: moderate σ (0.5-1.4) — bladed prismatic white-to-pale-green crystals; {110} cleavage at 56°/124°; classic non-asbestiform cabinet morphology.

habit_variants.name: massive_compact
habit_variants.trigger: low σ (< 0.5) — compact felted aggregate; low-relief surface mass.

tremolite.description: Ca₂Mg₅Si₈O₂₂(OH)₂ — monoclinic Ca-Mg calcic amphibole. H 5-6, density 3.0, vitreous luster, {110} prismatic cleavage at 56°/124°. Forms by metamorphism of impure dolomite (skarn — Manitoba + Lake Baikal + Italian Alps); rodingite-contact assemblages (Jeffrey Mine + Cassiar BC); talc deposits (the documented WHO-recognized health-hazard variety). ASBESTIFORM tremolite is one of six commercial asbestos minerals (the others: chrysotile, actinolite, anthophyllite, amosite, crocidolite). At JEFFREY MINE specifically, tremolite-asbestos is the documented chrysotile contaminant (WHO 1986 IARC Monograph; Frank et al. 2002 — more carcinogenic than chrysotile per epidemiological studies of Jeffrey workers). The mineral identity is geologically + scientifically important regardless of health context. Discriminator from analogous calcic amphiboles: tremolite is the Fe-poor Mg endmember; actinolite is the Fe-bearing intermediate (Fe > ~50 routes to actinolite). Refs: Hawthorne FC et al. (2012) Nomenclature of the amphibole supergroup. American Mineralogist 97:2031; Anthony Handbook v.IIB; Veblen DR & Wylie AG (1993) Mineralogy of amphiboles. Reviews in Mineralogy 28:61; WHO IARC Monograph 100C (2012) Asbestos.
tremolite._ingredients_note: Ca ≥ 60 (impure-dolomite Ca or rodingite Ca) + Mg ≥ 50 (the diagnostic — Mg-dominant calcic amphibole; Fe > 60 routes to actinolite) + SiO2 ≥ 250. T 200-700°C sweet spot 350-550. pH 7-12 alkaline.

trace_ingredients.Fe: trace gives faint green tint (intermediate toward actinolite end-member)
trace_ingredients.Cr: rare trace gives green chromian tremolite

tremolite.thermal_decomp_reaction: Above ~800°C: dehydration to diopside + enstatite + quartz + H2O.

fluorescence.status: Non-fluorescent.

twin_laws.name: simple_100
twin_laws._source: Hawthorne et al. 2012 Nomenclature of the amphibole supergroup (Amer Mineralogist 97:2031); Veblen & Wylie 1993 (Reviews in Mineralogy 28:61). The {100} simple twin is the standard amphibole-group growth twin (also reported as lamellar polysynthetic in deformed prograde-skarn specimens). Field frequency in bladed-prismatic specimens ~5-10%; conservative p=0.03 for the sample batch.

acid_dissolution.reaction: Ca2Mg5Si8O22(OH)2 + 14H⁺ → 2Ca²⁺ + 5Mg²⁺ + 8H4SiO4 — releases Ca + Mg + Si.

tremolite.audit_status: new v116 (2026-05-20). Calcic amphibole, first of the five commercial-asbestos amphiboles. Asbestiform habit dispatch (high-σ → fibrous wide-area mat). The documented Jeffrey Mine chrysotile contaminant per WHO. Per Hawthorne et al. 2012; Anthony Handbook v.IIB.
tremolite._late_stage_note: Early-to-mid stage in skarn + rodingite prograde sequences.

habit_variants.name: fibrous_asbestiform
habit_variants.trigger: high σ (excess > 1.4) — fibrous actinolite-asbestos green silky parallel bundles as a wall mat. WHO-recognized asbestos variety.

habit_variants.name: nephrite_compact
habit_variants.trigger: moderate σ (> 0.6) + cool T (< 350°C) + high Ca (> 100) — compact felted-fibrous actinolite mass; THE NEPHRITE-JADE GEMSTONE VARIETY (Maori pounamu + Chinese imperial jade). Translucent green.

habit_variants.name: bladed_columnar
habit_variants.trigger: moderate σ (0.5-1.4) — bladed prismatic green crystals; classic amphibole cabinet morphology.

habit_variants.name: massive_compact
habit_variants.trigger: low σ — massive granular green aggregate.

actinolite.description: Ca₂(Mg,Fe)₅Si₈O₂₂(OH)₂ — monoclinic Ca-Mg-Fe amphibole; Fe-bearing intermediate of the tremolite-actinolite series. H 5-6, density 3.0-3.4, green to dark-green characteristic color from Fe²⁺ M-site crystal field. Forms in greenschist-facies metamorphism + hydrothermal alteration + retrograde skarn. NEPHRITE-JADE variety (the more important of the two gem 'jade' minerals; jadeite is the other) is the compact felted-fibrous form — Maori pounamu (New Zealand), Chinese imperial jade (Hetian White Jade is the most famous actinolite nephrite), Cassiar BC + Wyoming USA + Italian Alps. Also the ACTINOLITE-ASBESTOS commercial variety (one of six WHO-recognized asbestos minerals). Trace Cr produces 'smaragdite' chromian-green actinolite (gem grade). Discriminator from tremolite: Fe ≥ ~30 ppm. Refs: Hawthorne FC et al. (2012) Am. Min. 97:2031; Wilson DGD & Yi-Lin S (2018) on nephrite jade chemistry; Anthony Handbook v.IIB.
actinolite._ingredients_note: Ca ≥ 60 + Mg ≥ 30 + Fe ≥ 30 (the diagnostic Fe-bearing intermediate) + SiO2 ≥ 250. T 200-700°C sweet spot 300-500. pH 6-11.

trace_ingredients.Cr: 1+ ppm trace gives emerald-green smaragdite (chromian actinolite gem variety)

actinolite.thermal_decomp_reaction: Above ~750°C: dehydration to clinopyroxene + olivine + quartz + H2O.

fluorescence.status: Non-fluorescent.

twin_laws.name: simple_lamellar_100
twin_laws._source: Anthony Handbook v.II (silicates) + Deer Howie Zussman 2nd ed. amphibole-group monograph. Actinolite (monoclinic C2/m, Ca2(Mg,Fe)5Si8O22(OH)2) shares the tremolite-actinolite series {100} simple + lamellar twin law. Tremolite already has twin_laws at v134 with the same law; this commit pairs them. Documented at Cziklowa (Romania), Ala (Italy), Wyoming Greenstone Belt. Conservative p=0.02 matching tremolite — most actinolite is untwinned bladed columnar.

acid_dissolution.reaction: Ca2(Mg,Fe)5Si8O22(OH)2 + 14H⁺ → 2Ca²⁺ + Mg²⁺ + Fe²⁺ + Si.

smaragdite_emerald.trigger: Cr > 1 ppm — chromian actinolite gem variety

nephrite_translucent_green.trigger: compact felted-fibrous habit at cool T + high Ca — the jade gem variety

actinolite.audit_status: new v116 (2026-05-20). Second of five commercial-asbestos amphiboles. Also THE major nephrite-jade gemstone variety. Cr trace dispatch for smaragdite green-gem variety. Per Hawthorne et al. 2012.

habit_variants.name: fibrous_asbestiform
habit_variants.trigger: high σ (excess > 0.8) — parallel-bundle brown-grey fibers; orthorhombic asbestiform anthophyllite (Finland + Carolina type); WHO-recognized asbestos variety.

habit_variants.name: prismatic_brown
habit_variants.trigger: moderate σ (0.4-0.8) — orthorhombic prismatic brown crystals; {210} cleavage.

habit_variants.name: massive_compact
habit_variants.trigger: low σ — compact brown-grey aggregate.

anthophyllite.description: (Mg,Fe)₇Si₈O₂₂(OH)₂ — ORTHORHOMBIC (Pnma) Mg-Fe orthoamphibole. NO Ca — discriminator vs the calcic amphiboles tremolite/actinolite. H 5.5-6, density 2.85-3.6, brown to grey-brown color. Forms in: ultramafic-host serpentinite-pegmatite-gneiss settings (Finland type — Kongsberg + Falun; Carolina + Georgia USA; Cyprus ophiolite); cordierite-anthophyllite gneisses (medium-grade metamorphic). COMMONLY ASBESTIFORM — anthophyllite-asbestos is one of six WHO-recognized commercial asbestos minerals (Cherokee NC + Bolt's Quarry Vermont were US commercial sources). Less commercially significant than chrysotile or crocidolite but historically used. The Carolina + Georgia anthophyllite-talc deposits are the historical US source. Discriminator from amosite: orthorhombic vs monoclinic; lower Fe-dominance. Refs: Hawthorne FC et al. (2012) Am. Min. 97:2031; Anthony Handbook v.IIB; Robinson P et al. (1971) — amphibole-talc rocks.
anthophyllite._ingredients_note: Mg ≥ 80 + Fe ≥ 30 + SiO2 ≥ 250. NO Ca gate but Ca > 80 routes to calcic amphibole (tremolite/actinolite wins). T 250-650°C sweet spot 350-550.

trace_ingredients.Al: moderate Al gives gedrite (anthophyllite-gedrite series end-member)

anthophyllite.thermal_decomp_reaction: Above ~750°C: dehydration to enstatite + olivine + quartz + H2O.

fluorescence.status: Non-fluorescent.

anthophyllite._twin_laws_note: Intentionally empty — anthophyllite (orthorhombic Pnma, (Mg,Fe)7Si8O22(OH)2) habit code 'fibrous_asbestiform' signals asbestos habit, fibers rather than individual euhedral amphibole crystals. The amphibole-group {100} twin law (carried by tremolite + actinolite) requires single crystals; asbestiform fibers don't admit it. v141 final batch.

acid_dissolution.reaction: (Mg,Fe)7Si8O22(OH)2 + 14H⁺ → 5Mg²⁺ + 2Fe²⁺ + 8H4SiO4.

anthophyllite.audit_status: new v116 (2026-05-20). Third of five commercial-asbestos amphiboles. NO-Ca orthoamphibole. Commonly asbestiform (Finland + Carolina type). Per Hawthorne et al. 2012.
anthophyllite._late_stage_note: Mid-stage in cordierite-anthophyllite gneisses + ultramafic-pegmatite contacts.

habit_variants.name: fibrous_asbestiform
habit_variants.trigger: high σ (excess > 0.4) — parallel-bundle brown silky fibers; cummingtonite-grunerite asbestos; "BROWN ASBESTOS" of South African commerce (Penge Transvaal type); WHO-recognized health-hazard variety.

habit_variants.name: prismatic_brown
habit_variants.trigger: low σ — prismatic brown amphibole crystals (rare non-asbestiform end).

amosite.description: ~(Fe,Mg)₇Si₈O₂₂(OH)₂ — Fe-DOMINANT cummingtonite-grunerite asbestiform variety. "BROWN ASBESTOS" of commerce. Type-locality Penge, Transvaal South Africa (also Cape Province + Witbank); minor production in India (Roro) and US (Montana). Monoclinic C2/m. H 5-6, density 3.1-3.6, brown-to-yellow-brown silky fiber bundles. One of six WHO-recognized commercial asbestos minerals; less carcinogenic than crocidolite but still classified as Group 1 carcinogen (IARC). The 'amosite' name is specifically the asbestiform commercial variety ("Asbestos Mines of South Africa"); non-asbestiform cummingtonite is a separate mineralogical name. Distinct from anthophyllite by structure (monoclinic vs orthorhombic) + by Fe-dominance. Refs: Hawthorne FC et al. (2012) Am. Min. 97:2031; Anthony Handbook v.IIB; Hodgson AA (1965) Mineralogy of South African amphibole asbestos. CSIR Report; Frank DR et al. (2002).
amosite._ingredients_note: Fe ≥ 100 (the diagnostic Fe-dominance; Fe/Mg > 1.2 routes here vs anthophyllite). NO Ca gate but Ca > 80 routes to calcic amphibole. SiO2 ≥ 250. T 200-500°C narrower than tremolite/actinolite (asbestiform variety has stricter low-grade-metamorphic window).
amosite.thermal_decomp_reaction: Above ~700°C: cummingtonite-grunerite dehydration to ferrosilite + iron-oxides + H2O.

fluorescence.status: Non-fluorescent.

amosite._twin_laws_note: Intentionally empty — amosite ((Fe,Mg)7Si8O22(OH)2, asbestiform variety of grunerite-cummingtonite amphibole series) habit code 'fibrous_asbestiform'. Like anthophyllite, the asbestiform habit precludes individual amphibole crystals that could carry the {100} twin law. v141 final batch.

acid_dissolution.reaction: Fe-cummingtonite + acid → 7Fe²⁺ + 8H4SiO4.

amosite.audit_status: new v116 (2026-05-20). Fourth of five commercial-asbestos amphiboles. "BROWN ASBESTOS" Fe-dominant cummingtonite-grunerite. Per Hawthorne et al. 2012; Hodgson 1965.

habit_variants.name: fibrous_asbestiform
habit_variants.trigger: high σ (excess > 0.4) — parallel-bundle deep blue silky fibers; riebeckite asbestos — "BLUE ASBESTOS"; the most carcinogenic asbestos variety per WHO + Frank et al. 2002; Wittenoom Australia + Northern Cape SA type material; PRECURSOR to tiger's eye gemstone via supergene oxidation.

habit_variants.name: massive_blue
habit_variants.trigger: low σ — massive blue-grey crocidolite mass (rare non-asbestiform).

crocidolite.description: Na₂Fe²⁺₃Fe³⁺₂Si₈O₂₂(OH)₂ — Na-Fe sodic amphibole (riebeckite-end variety with asbestiform habit). "BLUE ASBESTOS" of commerce. Monoclinic C2/m. H 4, density 3.2-3.4, deep blue to lavender-blue to slate-blue silky fiber bundles — the most distinctive color in the asbestos family. Type-locality association: Wittenoom Western Australia + Mount Brockman + Hamersley + Northern Cape South Africa (Asbestos Hills), all hosted in banded-iron-formation (BIF) — specifically the low-grade-metamorphic horizons where riebeckite recrystallizes asbestiform from primary BIF iron-silicates. Crocidolite is the MOST CARCINOGENIC of the six commercial asbestos minerals (Frank DR, Dodson RF, Williams MG 2002; the Wittenoom lawsuits closed the town in 1966 + dozens of lung-cancer/mesothelioma cases). PRECURSOR FOR TIGER'S EYE: supergene oxidation of crocidolite Fe²⁺ → Fe³⁺ at the surface, with chalcedony (SiO2) pseudomorphically replacing the fiber framework while preserving the chatoyant fiber-bundle morphology, produces the famous gold-brown chatoyant gemstone. Hawk's eye is the partial-oxidation intermediate (still blue-grey-gold). Refs: Hawthorne FC et al. (2012) Am. Min. 97:2031; Frank DR, Dodson RF, Williams MG (2002) Mineralogy and chemistry of South African crocidolite. International Journal of Occupational and Environmental Health 8:38; Hodgson AA (1965); Cairncross B & Beukes NJ (2013) on Northern Cape geology.
crocidolite._ingredients_note: Na ≥ 30 (the diagnostic Na — sodic amphibole vs calcic + Mg-Fe groups) + Fe ≥ 100 + SiO2 ≥ 200. T 100-400°C (low-grade BIF metamorphism). Ca > 50 routes to calcic amphibole. SUPERGENE OXIDATION (O2 > 0.4) dissolves crocidolite — Fe²⁺ → Fe³⁺, Na released to fluid, chalcedony replaces fiber framework producing tiger's eye pseudomorph.

trace_ingredients.Mn: trace can produce gold-toned crocidolite (hawk's eye precursor)

crocidolite.thermal_decomp_reaction: Above ~500°C: dehydration + oxidation to magnetite + hematite + quartz + albite + H2O. Riebeckite is the most thermally-unstable of the major amphiboles.

fluorescence.status: Non-fluorescent.

crocidolite._twin_laws_note: Intentionally empty — crocidolite (Na2Fe2+3Fe3+2Si8O22(OH)2, asbestiform variety of riebeckite) habit code 'fibrous_asbestiform' — blue asbestos. The fibrous-aligned riebeckite microfibers don't carry visible twinning. Parent riebeckite would; crocidolite as a habit-variant doesn't. v141 final batch.

acid_dissolution.reaction: Na2Fe5Si8O22(OH)2 + 12H⁺ → 2Na⁺ + 3Fe²⁺ + 2Fe³⁺ + 8H4SiO4. AT OXIDIZING pH > 5.5: in-situ Fe²⁺ → Fe³⁺ + Na release + chalcedony replacement = tiger's eye pseudomorph reaction.

hawks_eye_intermediate.trigger: O2 0.2-0.4 partial oxidation — blue-grey-gold transition toward tiger's eye

crocidolite.audit_status: new v116 (2026-05-20). Fifth and final of the commercial-asbestos amphiboles. "BLUE ASBESTOS" + most carcinogenic asbestos variety + PRECURSOR to tiger's eye gemstone via supergene oxidation. Per Hawthorne et al. 2012; Frank et al. 2002.
crocidolite._late_stage_note: BIF-hosted low-grade-metamorphic. The supergene-oxidation product (tiger's eye) is the famous gemstone; that's a separate engine in silicate class.

habit_variants.name: chatoyant_pseudomorph
habit_variants.trigger: default — gold-brown chatoyant chalcedony pseudomorph after crocidolite; silky cat's-eye effect from preserved fiber bundles; the CLASSIC gemstone aesthetic (Northern Cape SA + Wittenoom WA + Mt. Brockman type).

habit_variants.name: hawks_eye
habit_variants.trigger: blue-grey-gold partial-oxidation intermediate — crocidolite + chalcedony coexist; precursor stage to full tiger's eye.

habit_variants.name: tiger_iron
habit_variants.trigger: BIF-context — hematite + jasper + chalcedony banded mass; centimeter-scale interlayered red-black-gold bands; the Northern Cape SA + Hamersley WA "TIGER IRON" assemblage rock variety.

habit_variants.name: red_tigers_eye
habit_variants.trigger: red-brown to blood-red variant — higher Fe³⁺ chromophore loading; heat-altered tiger's eye (the gemstone trade calls this 'red tiger's eye'; sometimes natural, sometimes heat-treated).

tigers_eye.description: SiO₂ (microcrystalline chalcedony) PSEUDOMORPH AFTER crocidolite-asbestos. The famous gold-brown chatoyant gemstone. H 7, density 2.65, vitreous to silky luster, conchoidal fracture. NOT a separate mineral species mineralogically — tiger's eye is a VARIETY of chalcedony (microcrystalline SiO2) that has replaced crocidolite-asbestos fiber bundles via supergene oxidation, preserving the fibrous morphology to produce the diagnostic chatoyant cat's-eye optical effect. Type-locality association: Northern Cape South Africa (Asbestos Hills + Doornberg + Mount Brockman) BIF; also Hamersley + Wittenoom Western Australia; secondary occurrences worldwide (USA, India, China). The chatoyant golden-brown color is from Fe³⁺ in the chalcedony fiber framework, with iron oxides also dispersed in the silica matrix. THREE VARIETIES via habit dispatch (all gemstone-trade-recognized): tiger's eye proper (gold-brown chatoyant), hawk's eye (blue-grey-gold partial-oxidation intermediate where some crocidolite remains), tiger iron (BIF-banded assemblage with hematite + jasper layers between tiger's eye bands — Australian + South African occurrence; technically a ROCK not a mineral, but the gemstone trade markets it as a single name). Per Heaney PJ & Fisher DM (2003) Am. Min. 88:1-14 'New interpretation of the origin of tiger's-eye' — the modern mechanism is crocidolite replacement, NOT crocidolite-fiber-inclusions-in-quartz (the older interpretation). Refs: Heaney PJ & Fisher DM (2003) Am. Min. 88:1-14; Cairncross B & Beukes NJ (2013); Cogan SL et al. (2009) Tiger's eye formation in Australia.
tigers_eye._ingredients_note: SiO2 ≥ 200 (chalcedony framework) + Fe ≥ 30 (Fe³⁺ chromophore for chatoyant color). O2 > 0.4 STRICT oxidizing (Fe²⁺ → Fe³⁺ supergene weathering of crocidolite precursor). T 20-200°C surface-supergene. pH 5.5-9.5.
tigers_eye.thermal_decomp_reaction: Above ~870°C: chalcedony recrystallizes to quartz (loses chatoyancy). Heat-treatment to ~700°C gives red tiger's eye via Fe²⁺ → Fe³⁺ in the chalcedony fabric.

fluorescence.status: Non-fluorescent.

tigers_eye._twin_laws_note: Intentionally empty — tigers_eye is a chalcedony pseudomorph after crocidolite (asbestiform riebeckite) — it inherits the fibrous parallel-aligned fabric of the parent + the microcrystalline silica replacement texture. No euhedral crystal form, no twinning. The original crocidolite parent could in principle have had amphibole-group {100} twins, but those are obliterated by the chalcedony replacement.

acid_dissolution.reaction: SiO2 stable to acid; framework collapse only at pH < 4 + HF.

gold_brown.trigger: Fe³⁺ chatoyant chalcedony — the classic gemstone color

hawks_eye_blue_grey.trigger: partial crocidolite + chalcedony coexistence (intermediate oxidation state)

red_brown.trigger: higher Fe³⁺ loading or heat-altered — 'red tiger's eye'

tiger_iron_banded.trigger: BIF context — banded with hematite + jasper layers

tigers_eye.audit_status: new v116 (2026-05-20). Chalcedony pseudomorph after crocidolite — the famous gold-brown chatoyant gemstone. Substrate priority: crocidolite_dissolving (p=0.65, the canonical paragenesis) > hematite (p=0.45, tiger iron BIF context) > magnetite > wall. Three habit variants cover the gemstone family (chatoyant_pseudomorph default, hawks_eye partial-oxidation, tiger_iron BIF-banded). Per Heaney & Fisher 2003 Am.Min. 88:1.
tigers_eye._late_stage_note: Very late — supergene surface-weathering product. By definition fires AFTER crocidolite forms + is exposed to oxidizing conditions.
